I need precise file paths and line numbers for several related definitions. Please do a "very thorough" search and report back with file paths and line numbers (and a 1-2 line description) for each of these:

1. The `ViewBinding` and `AggregateViewBinding` case classes / classes (likely in core/src/main/scala/chisel3/internal/binding/ somewhere). Show their constructor fields.
2. The `ViewWriteability` sealed trait/class and its subclasses (Default, ReadOnly, ReadOnlyDeprecated). Show the `combine` and `isReadOnly` methods and their line numbers.
3. The `ViewParent` object (likely in Builder.scala or similar) — what it represents.

Where `reify` is CALLED — search the codebase for `dataview.reify` or `.reify(` calls, especially in connection logic (MonoConnect.scala, BiConnect.scala, connectable/ package), in Aggregate.scala (for `viewAs`/connect), and in Data.scala. Report the call sites with file:line and a short note on what the reified result is used for.
6. The `viewAs` method on Data (the public user-facing API). Is there a `def viewAs` on Data.scala or Aggregate.scala? Where exactly?
7. `Builder.viewNamespace` — where defined.
8. Any example/test files demonstrating DataView usage, especially a Bundle-to-UInt reinterpreter view. Look in core/src/test and src/test for files named *DataView*. Report the file path and a representative small example snippet (the actual code), with line numbers.

Report findings as a list with absolute file paths and line numbers. Do NOT modify any files. Be precise about line numbers — these will become permanent links.

211	            key.binding match {

232	        .toVector
233	        .distinct
234	      if (contexts.size == 1) Some(contexts.head)

5	import chisel3.experimental.BaseModule
6	import chisel3.{getRecursiveFields, Data, Vec}

8	import scala.annotation.implicitNotFound

10	/** Typeclass interface for getting elements of type [[Data]]
11	  *
12	  * This is needed for validating [[DataView]]s targeting type `A`.
13	  * Can be thought of as "can be the Target of a DataView".
14	  *
15	  * Chisel provides some implementations in [[DataProduct$ object DataProduct]] that are available
16	  * by default in the implicit scope.
17	  *
18	  * @tparam A Type that has elements of type [[Data]]
19	  * @see [[https://www.chisel-lang.org/chisel3/docs/explanations/dataview#dataproduct Detailed Documentation]]
20	  */
21	@implicitNotFound(
22	  "Could not find implicit value for DataProduct[${A}].\n" +
23	    "Please see https://www.chisel-lang.org/chisel3/docs/explanations/dataview#dataproduct"
24	)
25	trait DataProduct[-A] {
26	
27	  /** Provides [[Data]] elements within some containing object
28	    *
29	    * @param a Containing object
30	    * @param path Hierarchical path to current signal (for error reporting)
31	    * @return Data elements and associated String paths (Strings for error reporting only!)
32	    */
33	  def dataIterator(a: A, path: String): Iterator[(Data, String)]
34	
35	  /** Returns a checker to test if the containing object contains a `Data` object
36	    * @note Implementers may want to override if iterating on all `Data` is expensive for `A` and `A`
37	    *       will primarily be used in `PartialDataViews`
38	    * @note The returned value is a function, not a true Set, but is describing the functionality of
39	    *       Set containment
40	    * @param a Containing object
41	    * @return A checker that itself returns True if a given `Data` is contained in `a`
42	    *         as determined by an `==` test
43	    */
44	  def dataSet(a: A): Data => Boolean = dataIterator(a, "").map(_._1).toSet
45	}
46	
47	/** Low priority built-in implementations of [[DataProduct]]
48	  *
49	  * @note This trait exists so that `dataDataProduct` can be lower priority than `iterableDataProduct` to resolve ambiguity
50	  */
51	sealed trait LowPriorityDataProduct {
52	
53	  /** [[DataProduct]] implementation for [[Data]] */
54	  implicit val dataDataProduct: DataProduct[Data] = new DataProduct[Data] {
55	    def dataIterator(a: Data, path: String): Iterator[(Data, String)] =
56	      getRecursiveFields.lazily(a, path).iterator
57	  }
58	}
59	
60	/** Encapsulating object for built-in implementations of [[DataProduct]]
61	  *
62	  * @note DataProduct implementations provided in this object are available in the implicit scope
63	  */
64	object DataProduct extends LowPriorityDataProduct {
65	
66	  /** Factory method for constructing [[DataProduct]]s */
67	  def apply[A](f: (A, String) => Iterator[(Data, String)]): DataProduct[A] = new DataProduct[A] {
68	    def dataIterator(a: A, path: String): Iterator[(Data, String)] = f(a, path)
69	  }
70	
71	  /** Factory for constructing [[DataProduct]] for types that do not contain any [[Data]] */
72	  def empty[A]: DataProduct[A] = apply[A] { case _ => Iterator.empty }
73	
74	  // DataProducts for simple primitive types
75	  implicit val charDataProduct:   DataProduct[Char] = empty
76	  implicit val stringDataProduct: DataProduct[String] = empty
77	  implicit val intDataProduct:    DataProduct[Int] = empty
78	  implicit val byteDataProduct:   DataProduct[Byte] = empty
79	  implicit val shortDataProduct:  DataProduct[Short] = empty
80	  implicit val longDataProduct:   DataProduct[Long] = empty
81	  implicit val bigIntDataProduct: DataProduct[BigInt] = empty
82	  implicit val floatDataProduct:  DataProduct[Float] = empty
83	  implicit val doubleDataProduct: DataProduct[Double] = empty
84	
85	  /** [[DataProduct]] implementation for [[BaseModule]] */
86	  implicit val userModuleDataProduct: DataProduct[BaseModule] = new DataProduct[BaseModule] {
87	    def dataIterator(a: BaseModule, path: String): Iterator[(Data, String)] = {
88	      a.getIds.iterator.flatMap {
89	        case d: Data if d.getOptionRef.isDefined => // Using ref to decide if it's truly hardware in the module
90	          implicitly[DataProduct[Data]].dataIterator(d, s"${path}.${d.instanceName}")
91	        case b: BaseModule => dataIterator(b, s"$path.${b.instanceName}")
92	        case _ => Seq.empty
93	      }
94	    }
95	    // Overridden for performance
96	    override def dataSet(a: BaseModule): Data => Boolean = {
97	      val lastId = a._lastId // Not cheap to compute
98	      // Return a function
99	      e => e._id > a._id && e._id <= lastId
100	    }
101	  }

103	  /** [[DataProduct]] implementation for any `Seq[A]` where `A` has an implementation of `DataProduct`. */
104	  @deprecated("Use iterableDataProduct instead", "Chisel 7.0")
105	  def seqDataProduct[A: DataProduct]: DataProduct[Seq[A]] = new DataProduct[Seq[A]] {
106	    def dataIterator(a: Seq[A], path: String): Iterator[(Data, String)] = {
107	      val dpa = implicitly[DataProduct[A]]
108	      a.iterator.zipWithIndex.flatMap { case (elt, idx) =>

110	      }

120	      }

122	  }

124	  /** [[DataProduct]] implementation for any [[scala.Tuple2]] where each field has an implementation of `DataProduct`. */
125	  implicit def tuple2DataProduct[A: DataProduct, B: DataProduct]: DataProduct[(A, B)] = new DataProduct[(A, B)] {
126	    def dataIterator(tup: (A, B), path: String): Iterator[(Data, String)] = {

128	      val dpb = implicitly[DataProduct[B]]

135	  implicit def tuple3DataProduct[A: DataProduct, B: DataProduct, C: DataProduct]: DataProduct[(A, B, C)] =
136	    new DataProduct[(A, B, C)] {
137	      def dataIterator(tup: (A, B, C), path: String): Iterator[(Data, String)] = {
138	        val dpa = implicitly[DataProduct[A]]

19	  @implicitNotFound("You are trying to access a macro-only API. Please use the @public annotation instead.")
20	  trait MacroGenerated
21	
22	  /** Marker trait for modules that are not true modules */
23	  private[chisel3] trait PseudoModule extends BaseModule
24	
25	  /* Check if a String name is a temporary name */
26	  def isTemp(name: String): Boolean = name.nonEmpty && name.head == '_'
27	
28	  /** Creates a name String from a prefix and a seed
29	    * @param prefix The prefix associated with the seed (must be in correct order, *not* reversed)
30	    * @param seed The seed for computing the name (if available)
31	    */
32	  def buildName(seed: String, prefix: Prefix): String = {
33	    // Don't bother copying the String if there's no prefix
34	    if (prefix.isEmpty) {
35	      seed
36	    } else {
37	      // Using Java's String builder to micro-optimize appending a String excluding 1st character
38	      // for temporaries
39	      val builder = new java.lang.StringBuilder()
40	      // Starting with _ is the indicator of a temporary
41	      val temp = isTemp(seed)
42	      // Make sure the final result is also a temporary if this is a temporary
43	      if (temp) {
44	        builder.append('_')
45	      }
46	      prefix.foreach { p =>
47	        // Don't append _ if the prefix is empty
48	        if (p.nonEmpty) {
49	          builder.append(p)
50	          builder.append('_')
51	        }
52	      }
53	      if (temp) {
54	        // We've moved the leading _ to the front, drop it here
55	        builder.append(seed, 1, seed.length)
56	      } else {
57	        builder.append(seed)
58	      }
59	      builder.toString
60	    }
61	  }

63	  // Sanitizes a name, e.g. from a `HasId`, by stripping all non ANSI-C characters
64	  private[chisel3] def sanitize(s: String, leadingDigitOk: Boolean = false): String = {
65	    // TODO what character set does FIRRTL truly support? using ANSI C for now
66	    def legalStart(c: Char) = (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') || c == '_'
67	    def legal(c:      Char) = legalStart(c) || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
68	    val res = if (s.forall(legal)) s else s.filter(legal)
69	    val headOk = (!res.isEmpty) && (leadingDigitOk || legalStart(res.head))
70	    if (headOk) res else s"_$res"
71	  }

73	  // Workaround for https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/issues/4162
74	  // We can't use the .asTypeOf workaround because this is used to implement .asTypeOf
75	  private[chisel3] def _padHandleBool[A <: Bits](
76	    x:      A,
77	    width:  Int,
78	    isUInt: Boolean
79	  )(
80	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
81	  ): A = x match {
82	    case b: Bool if !b.isLit && width > 1 && isUInt =>
83	      val _pad = Wire(UInt(width.W))
84	      _pad := b
85	      _pad.asInstanceOf[A] // This cast is safe because we know A is UInt on this path
86	    case u => u.pad(width).asInstanceOf[A]
87	  }
88	
89	  // Resize that to this width (if known)
90	  private[chisel3] def _resizeToWidth[A <: Bits](
91	    that:           A,
92	    targetWidthOpt: Option[Int],
93	    isUInt:         Boolean
94	  )(fromUInt: UInt => A)(
95	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
96	  ): A = {
97	    (targetWidthOpt, that.widthOption) match {
98	      case (Some(targetWidth), Some(thatWidth)) =>
99	        if (targetWidth == thatWidth) that
100	        else if (targetWidth > thatWidth) _padHandleBool(that, targetWidth, isUInt)
101	        else fromUInt(that.take(targetWidth))
102	      case (Some(targetWidth), None) => fromUInt(_padHandleBool(that, targetWidth, isUInt).take(targetWidth))
103	      case (None, Some(thatWidth))   => that
104	      case (None, None)              => that
105	    }
106	  }

208	  private[chisel3] implicit class ListSyntax[A](xs: List[A]) {
209	    def mapAccumulate[B, C](z: B)(f: (B, A) => (B, C)): (B, List[C]) = {
210	      val (zz, result) = xs.foldLeft((z, List.empty[C])) { case ((acc, res), a) =>
211	        val (accx, c) = f(acc, a)
212	        (accx, c :: res)
213	      }
214	      (zz, result.reverse)
215	    }
216	  }

268	  /** Similar to Seq.groupBy except that it preserves ordering of elements within each group */
269	  private[chisel3] def groupByIntoSeq[A, K](xs: Iterable[A])(f: A => K): Seq[(K, Seq[A])] = {
270	    val map = mutable.LinkedHashMap.empty[K, mutable.ListBuffer[A]]
271	    for (x <- xs) {
272	      val key = f(x)
273	      val l = map.getOrElseUpdate(key, mutable.ListBuffer.empty[A])
274	      l += x
275	    }
276	    map.view.map({ case (k, vs) => k -> vs.toList }).toList
277	  }

12	/** Mapping between a target type `T` and a view type `V`

15	  *
16	  * ==Detailed documentation==
17	  *   - [[https://www.chisel-lang.org/chisel3/docs/explanations/dataview Explanation]]
18	  *   - [[https://www.chisel-lang.org/chisel3/docs/cookbooks/dataview Cookbook]]
19	  *
20	  * @example {{{
21	  * class Foo(val w: Int) extends Bundle {
22	  *   val a = UInt(w.W)
23	  * }
24	  * class Bar(val w: Int) extends Bundle {
25	  *   val b = UInt(w.W)
26	  * }
27	  * // DataViews are created using factory methods in the companion object
28	  * implicit val view = DataView[Foo, Bar](
29	  *   // The first argument is a function constructing a Foo from a Bar
30	  *   foo => new Bar(foo.w)
31	  *   // The remaining arguments are a variable number of field pairings
32	  *   _.a -> _.b
33	  * )
34	  * }}}
35	  *
36	  * @tparam T Target type (must have an implementation of [[DataProduct]])
37	  * @tparam V View type
38	  * @see [[DataView$ object DataView]] for factory methods
39	  * @see [[PartialDataView object PartialDataView]] for defining non-total `DataViews`
40	  */
41	@implicitNotFound(

43	    "Please see https://www.chisel-lang.org/chisel3/docs/explanations/dataview"
44	)
45	sealed class DataView[T: DataProduct, V <: Data] private[chisel3] (
46	  /** Function constructing an object of the View type from an object of the Target type */
47	  private[chisel3] val mkView: T => V,

22	  implicit class DataViewable[T](target: T) {

35	      requireIsChiselType(result, "viewAs")
36	
37	      doBind(target, result, dataView, writability)
38	
39	      // Setting the parent marks these Data as Views
40	      result.setAllParents(Some(ViewParent))
41	      // The names of views do not matter except for when a view is annotated. For Views that correspond
42	      // To a single Data, we just forward the name of the Target. For Views that correspond to more
43	      // than one Data, we return this assigned name but rename it in the Convert stage
44	      result._forceName("view", Builder.viewNamespace)
45	      result
46	    }

49	      implicit dataproduct: DataProduct[T],

81	  private def nonTotalViewException(
82	    dataView:     DataView[_, _],
83	    target:       Any,
84	    view:         Data,
85	    targetFields: Seq[String],
86	    viewFields:   Seq[String]
87	  ) = {
88	    def missingMsg(name: String, fields: Seq[String]): Option[String] = {
89	      val str = fields.mkString(", ")
90	      fields.size match {
91	        case 0 => None
92	        case 1 => Some(s"$name field '$str' is missing")
93	        case _ => Some(s"$name fields '$str' are missing")
94	      }
95	    }
96	    val vs = missingMsg("view", viewFields)
97	    val ts = missingMsg("target", targetFields)
98	    val reasons = (ts ++ vs).mkString(" and ").capitalize
99	    val suggestion = if (ts.nonEmpty) "\n  If the view *should* be non-total, try a 'PartialDataView'." else ""
100	    val msg = s"Viewing $target as $view is non-Total!\n  $reasons.\n  DataView used is $dataView.$suggestion"
101	    throw InvalidViewException(msg)
102	  }
103	
104	  // TODO should this be moved to class Aggregate / can it be unified with Aggregate.bind?
105	  private def doBind[T: DataProduct, V <: Data](
106	    target:      T,
107	    view:        V,
108	    dataView:    DataView[T, V],
109	    writability: ViewWriteability
110	  )(
111	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
112	  ): Unit = {
113	    val mapping = dataView.mapping(target, view)
114	    val total = dataView.total
115	    // Lookups to check the mapping results
116	    val viewFieldLookup: Map[Data, String] = getRecursiveFields(view, "_").toMap
117	    val targetContains:  Data => Boolean = implicitly[DataProduct[T]].dataSet(target)
118	
119	    // Resulting bindings for each Element of the View
120	    // Kept separate from Aggregates for totality checking
121	    val elementBindings =
122	      new mutable.LinkedHashMap[Data, mutable.ListBuffer[Element]] ++
123	        getRecursiveFields
124	          .lazilyNoPath(view)
125	          .collect { case (elt: Element) => elt }
126	          .map(_ -> new mutable.ListBuffer[Element])
127	
128	    // Record any Aggregates that correspond 1:1 for reification
129	    // Using Data instead of Aggregate to avoid unnecessary checks
130	    val aggregateMappings = mutable.ArrayBuffer.empty[(Data, Data)]
131	
132	    def viewFieldName(d: Data): String =
133	      viewFieldLookup.get(d).map(_ + " ").getOrElse("") + d.toString
134	
135	    // Helper for recording the binding of each
136	    def onElt(te: Element, ve: Element): Unit = {
137	      // TODO can/should we aggregate these errors?
138	      def err(name: String, arg: Data) =
139	        throw InvalidViewException(s"View mapping must only contain Elements within the $name, got $arg")
140	
141	      // The elements may themselves be views, look through the potential chain of views for the Elements
142	      // that are actually members of the target or view
143	      val tex = unfoldView(te).find(x => targetContains(x) || x.isLit || x == DontCare).getOrElse(err("Target", te))
144	      val vex = unfoldView(ve).find(viewFieldLookup.contains).getOrElse(err("View", ve))
145	      if (!tex.isSynthesizable) {
146	        Builder.exception(s".viewAs should only be called on hardware")
147	      }
148	
149	      (tex, vex) match {
150	        /* Allow views where the types are equal. */
151	        case (a, b) if a.getClass == b.getClass =>
152	          // View width must be unknown or match target width
153	          if (vex.widthKnown && vex.width != tex.width) {
154	            def widthAsString(x: Element) = x.widthOption.map("<" + _ + ">").getOrElse("<unknown>")
155	            val fieldName = viewFieldName(vex)
156	            val vwidth = widthAsString(vex)
157	            val twidth = widthAsString(tex)
158	            throw InvalidViewException(
159	              s"View field $fieldName has width ${vwidth} that is incompatible with target value $tex's width ${twidth}"
160	            )
161	          }
162	        /* allow bool <=> reset views. */
163	        case (a: Bool, _: Reset) =>
164	        case (_: Reset, a: Bool) =>
165	        /* Allow AsyncReset <=> Reset views. */
166	        case (a: AsyncReset, _: Reset) =>
167	        case (_: Reset, a: AsyncReset) =>
168	        /* Allow DontCare in the target only */
169	        case (DontCare, _) =>
170	        /* Allow Property[_] <=> Property[_] views when the underlying type is the same */
171	        case (a: Property[_], b: Property[_]) if a.getPropertyType == b.getPropertyType =>
172	        /* All other views produce a runtime error. */
173	        case _ =>
174	          val fieldName = viewFieldName(vex)
175	          throw InvalidViewException(s"Field $fieldName specified as view of non-type-equivalent value $tex")
176	      }
177	
178	      elementBindings(vex) += tex
179	    }
180	
181	    mapping.foreach {
182	      // Special cased because getMatchedFields checks typeEquivalence on Elements (and is used in Aggregate path)
183	      // Also saves object allocations on common case of Elements
184	      case (ae: Element, be: Element) => onElt(ae, be)
185	
186	      case (aa: Aggregate, ba: Aggregate) =>
187	        if (!ba.typeEquivalent(aa)) {
188	          val fieldName = viewFieldLookup(ba)
189	          val reason = ba
190	            .findFirstTypeMismatch(aa, strictTypes = true, strictWidths = true, strictProbeInfo = true)
191	            .map(s => s"\nbecause $s")
192	            .getOrElse("")
193	          throw InvalidViewException(
194	            s"Field $fieldName specified as view of non-type-equivalent value $aa$reason"
195	          )
196	        }
197	        getMatchedFields(aa, ba).foreach {
198	          case (aelt: Element, belt: Element) => onElt(aelt, belt)
199	          case (t, v)                         => aggregateMappings += (v -> t)
200	        }
201	    }
202	
203	    // Errors in totality of the View, use var List to keep fast path cheap (no allocation)
204	    var viewNonTotalErrors:   List[Data] = Nil
205	    var targetNonTotalErrors: List[String] = Nil
206	
207	    val targetSeen: Option[mutable.Set[Data]] = if (total) Some(mutable.Set.empty[Data]) else None
208	
209	    val elementResult = elementBindings.map { case (data, targets) =>
210	      val targetsx = targets match {
211	        case collection.Seq(target: Element) => target
212	        case collection.Seq() =>
213	          viewNonTotalErrors = data :: viewNonTotalErrors
214	          data.asInstanceOf[Element] // Return the Data itself, will error after this map, cast is safe
215	        case x =>
216	          throw InvalidViewException(s"Got $x, expected Seq(_: Direct)")
217	      }
218	      // TODO record and report aliasing errors
219	      targetSeen.foreach(_ += targetsx)
220	      data -> targetsx
221	    }.toMap
222	
223	    // Check for totality of Target
224	    targetSeen.foreach { seen =>
225	      val lookup = implicitly[DataProduct[T]].dataIterator(target, "_")
226	      for (missed <- lookup.collect { case (d: Element, name) if !seen(d) => name }) {
227	        targetNonTotalErrors = missed :: targetNonTotalErrors
228	      }
229	    }
230	    if (viewNonTotalErrors != Nil || targetNonTotalErrors != Nil) {
231	      val viewErrors = viewNonTotalErrors.map(f => viewFieldLookup.getOrElse(f, f.toString))
232	      nonTotalViewException(dataView, target, view, targetNonTotalErrors, viewErrors)
233	    }
234	
235	    view match {
236	      case elt: Element => view.bind(ViewBinding(elementResult(elt), writability))
237	      case agg: Aggregate =>
238	        val fullResult = elementResult ++ aggregateMappings
239	        val aggWritability = Option.when(writability.isReadOnly)(
240	          Map((agg: Data) -> writability)
241	        )
242	        agg.bind(AggregateViewBinding(fullResult, aggWritability))
243	    }
244	  }
245	
246	  // When annotating views that are not identity mappings, we need to record them for renaming
247	  // Technically, this adds any Aggregate that is not an identity mapping,
248	  // but we don't have a cheap way to check for single-target.
249	  private[chisel3] def recordViewForRenaming(view: Data): Unit = {
250	    view.topBinding match {
251	      case _: ViewBinding => () // No need for renaming
252	      case AggregateViewBinding(mapping, _) =>
253	        getRecursiveFields.lazily(view, "_").foreach {
254	          case (unnamed: Aggregate, _) if !mapping.contains(unnamed) =>
255	            Builder.unnamedViews += unnamed
256	          case _ => () // Do nothing
257	        }
258	      case _ =>
259	        Builder.exception(s"Internal Error! recordViewForRenaming($view)")(UnlocatableSourceInfo)
260	    }
261	  }

263	  // Traces an Element that may (or may not) be a view until it no longer maps
264	  // Inclusive of the argument
265	  // Note that this does *not* include writability so do not use this in place of reify.
266	  private def unfoldView(elt: Element): LazyList[Element] = {
267	    def rec(e: Element): LazyList[Element] = e.topBindingOpt match {
268	      case Some(ViewBinding(target, _)) => target #:: rec(target)
269	      case Some(avb: AggregateViewBinding) =>
270	        val target = avb.lookup(e).get
271	        target #:: rec(target)
272	      case Some(_) | None => LazyList.empty
273	    }
274	    elt #:: rec(elt)
275	  }

277	  // Safe for all Data
278	  private[chisel3] def isView(d: Data): Boolean = d._parent.exists(_ == ViewParent)
279	
280	  /** Turn any [[Element]] that could be a View into a concrete Element
281	    *
282	    * This is the fundamental "unwrapping" or "tracing" primitive operation for handling Views within
283	    * Chisel.
284	    */
285	  private[chisel3] def reify(elt: Element): (Element, ViewWriteability) =
286	    reify(elt, elt.topBinding, ViewWriteability.Default)
287	
288	  /** Turn any [[Element]] that could be a View into a concrete Element
289	    *
290	    * This is the fundamental "unwrapping" or "tracing" primitive operation for handling Views within
291	    * Chisel.
292	    */
293	  @tailrec private[chisel3] def reify(
294	    elt:        Element,
295	    topBinding: TopBinding,
296	    wrAcc:      ViewWriteability
297	  ): (Element, ViewWriteability) = {
298	    topBinding match {
299	      case ViewBinding(target, writeability) =>
300	        reify(target, target.topBinding, wrAcc.combine(writeability))
301	      case _ => (elt, wrAcc)
302	    }
303	  }
304	
305	  /** Determine the target of a View if the view is an identity mapping.
306	    *
307	    * This is only true if the target of the view is of the same type and fields correspond 1:1.
308	    * For example, it would *not* be an identity view to view a Vec as a Vec with its elements in reverse order.
309	    *
310	    * @return The identity target of this view or None if not an identity view.
311	    * @note Returns Some(_) of the argument if it is not a view.
312	    */
313	  private[chisel3] def reifyIdentityView[T <: Data](
314	    data:  T,
315	    wrAcc: ViewWriteability = ViewWriteability.Default
316	  ): Option[(T, ViewWriteability)] = {
317	    val candidate: Option[(Data, ViewWriteability)] =
318	      data.topBindingOpt match {
319	        case None                                       => None
320	        case Some(ViewBinding(target, wr))              => Some(target -> wr)
321	        case Some(vb @ AggregateViewBinding(lookup, _)) => lookup.get(data).map(_ -> vb.lookupWritability(data))
322	        case Some(_)                                    => Some(data -> ViewWriteability.Default)
323	      }
324	    candidate.flatMap { case (d, wr) =>
325	      val wrx = wrAcc.combine(wr)
326	      // This cast is safe by construction, we only put Data in the view mapping if it is an identity mapping.
327	      val cast = d.asInstanceOf[T]
328	      // Candidate may itself be a view, keep tracing in those cases.
329	      if (isView(d)) reifyIdentityView(cast, wrx) else Some(cast -> wrx)
330	    }
331	  }
332	
333	  // Return all parents of a Data, including itself
334	  private def allParents(d: Data): List[Data] = d.binding match {
335	    case Some(ChildBinding(parent)) => d :: allParents(parent)
336	    case _                          => List(d)
337	  }
338	
339	  /** Determine the target of a View if the view maps to a single `Data`.
340	    *
341	    * An Aggregate may be a view of `non-Data` (like a `Seq` or tuple) and thus
342	    * there is no single Data representing the Target and this function will return None.
343	    *
344	    * @return The single Data target of this view or None if a single Data doesn't exist.
345	    * @note Returns Some(_) of the argument if it is not a view.
346	    * @note You should never attempt to write the result of this function.
347	    */
348	  private[chisel3] def reifySingleTarget(data: Data): Option[Data] = {
349	    def err(msg: String) = throwException(s"Internal Error! $msg reifySingleTarget($data)")
350	    // Identity views are obviously single targets.
351	    // We ignore writability because the return of this function should never be written.
352	    reifyIdentityView(data).map(_._1).orElse {
353	      // Otherwise, all children of data need to map to all of the children of another Data.
354	      // This is really expensive, is there a better way?
355	      data.topBindingOpt.flatMap {
356	        case AggregateViewBinding(mapping, _) =>
357	          // Take every single leaf and map to its target
358	          val leaves = DataMirror.collectLeafMembers(data)
359	          val targets = leaves.map { l =>
360	            // All leaves are stored in the mapping.
361	            val oneLevel = mapping(l)
362	            // This .get is safe because collectLeafMembers returns leaves.
363	            // It is of type Data (not Element) because of Probes, but Probes are atomic.
364	            reifySingleTarget(oneLevel).get
365	          }
366	          // Now, if there are any targets, check if all of the targets share a common parent,
367	          // and if that parent is exclusively composed of these targets.
368	          val tset = targets.toSet
369	          targets.headOption.flatMap { head =>
370	            allParents(head).find {
371	              // This is kind of a hack but ClonePorts is itself a hack.
372	              // We must ignore ClonePorts because it isn't a real Data, so it cannot be the "Single Target" to which we map.
373	              case _: ClonePorts => false
374	              case p =>
375	                val pset = DataMirror.collectLeafMembers(p).toSet
376	                pset == tset
377	            }
378	          }
379	        // Anything else should've been handled by reifyIdentityView
380	        case bad => err(s"This should not be reachable. Got binding = $bad in")
381	
382	      }
383	    }
384	  }
385	}

18	/**
19	  * BiConnect.connect executes a bidirectional connection element-wise.
20	  *
21	  * Note that the arguments are left and right (not source and sink) so the
22	  * intent is for the operation to be commutative.
23	  *
24	  * The connect operation will recurse down the left Data (with the right Data).
25	  * An exception will be thrown if a movement through the left cannot be matched
26	  * in the right (or if the right side has extra fields).
27	  *
28	  * See elemConnect for details on how the root connections are issued.
29	  */
30	
31	private[chisel3] object BiConnect {
32	  // These are all the possible exceptions that can be thrown.
33	  // These are from element-level connection
34	  def BothDriversException =
35	    BiConnectException(": Both Left and Right are drivers")
36	  def NeitherDriverException =
37	    BiConnectException(": Neither Left nor Right is a driver")
38	  def UnknownDriverException =
39	    BiConnectException(": Locally unclear whether Left or Right (both internal)")
40	  def UnknownRelationException =
41	    BiConnectException(": Left or Right unavailable to current module.")
42	  // These are when recursing down aggregate types
43	  def MismatchedVecException =
44	    BiConnectException(": Left and Right are different length Vecs.")
45	  def MissingLeftFieldException(field: String) =
46	    BiConnectException(s".$field: Left Record missing field ($field).")
47	  def MissingRightFieldException(field: String) =
48	    BiConnectException(s": Right Record missing field ($field).")
49	  def MismatchedException(left: String, right: String) =
50	    BiConnectException(s": Left ($left) and Right ($right) have different types.")
51	  def AttachAlreadyBulkConnectedException(
52	    analog:         String,
53	    prevConnection: String,
54	    newConnection:  String,
55	    sourceInfo:     SourceInfo
56	  ) =
57	    BiConnectException(
58	      sourceInfo.makeMessage(
59	        s": Analog $analog previously bulk to $prevConnection is connected to $newConnection at " + _
60	      )
61	    )
62	  def DontCareCantBeSink =
63	    BiConnectException(": DontCare cannot be a connection sink (LHS)")
64	  def LeftProbeBiConnectionException(left: Data) =
65	    BiConnectException(s"Left of Probed type cannot participate in a bi connection (<>)")
66	  def RightProbeBiConnectionException(right: Data) =
67	    BiConnectException(s"Right of Probed type cannot participate in a bi connection (<>)")
68	
69	  private def collectNotReadOnly[T <: Data](x: Option[(T, ViewWriteability)]): Option[T] = {
70	    x.collect { case (z, ViewWriteability.Default) => z }
71	  }
72	
73	  /** This function is what recursively tries to connect a left and right together
74	    *
75	    * There is some cleverness in the use of internal try-catch to catch exceptions
76	    * during the recursive descent and then rethrow them with extra information added.
77	    * This gives the user a 'path' to where in the connections things went wrong.
78	    *
79	    * == Chisel Semantics and how they emit to firrtl ==
80	    *
81	    * 1. FIRRTL Connect (all fields as seen by firrtl must match exactly)
82	    *   `a <= b`
83	    *
84	    * 2. FIRRTL Connect (implemented as being field-blasted because we know all firrtl fields would not match exactly)
85	    *   `a.foo <= b.foo, b.bar <= a.bar`
86	    *
87	    * - The decision on 1 vs 2 is based on structural type -- if same type once emitted to firrtl, emit 1, otherwise emit 2
88	    */
89	  def connect(
90	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
91	    left:        Data,
92	    right:       Data,
93	    context_mod: RawModule
94	  ): Unit = {
95	    (left, right) match {
96	      // Disallow monoconnecting Probe types
97	      case (left_e: Data, _) if containsProbe(left_e) =>
98	        throw LeftProbeBiConnectionException(left_e)
99	      case (_, right_e: Data) if containsProbe(right_e) =>
100	        throw RightProbeBiConnectionException(right_e)
101	
102	      // Handle element case (root case)
103	      case (left_a: Analog, right_a: Analog) =>
104	        try {
105	          markAnalogConnected(sourceInfo, left_a, right_a, context_mod)
106	          markAnalogConnected(sourceInfo, right_a, left_a, context_mod)
107	        } catch { // convert attach exceptions to BiConnectExceptions
108	          case attach.AttachException(message) => throw BiConnectException(message)
109	        }
110	        attach.impl(Seq(left_a, right_a), context_mod)(sourceInfo)
111	      case (left_a: Analog, DontCare) =>
112	        try {
113	          markAnalogConnected(sourceInfo, left_a, DontCare, context_mod)
114	        } catch { // convert attach exceptions to BiConnectExceptions
115	          case attach.AttachException(message) => throw BiConnectException(message)
116	        }
117	        pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, left_a.lref(sourceInfo)))
118	      case (DontCare, right_a: Analog) => connect(sourceInfo, right, left, context_mod)
119	      case (left_e: Element, right_e: Element) => {
120	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, left_e, right_e, context_mod)
121	        // TODO(twigg): Verify the element-level classes are connectable
122	      }
123	      // Handle Vec case
124	      case (left_v: Vec[Data @unchecked], right_v: Vec[Data @unchecked]) => {
125	        if (left_v.length != right_v.length) {
126	          throw MismatchedVecException
127	        }
128	
129	        // Filter out read-only because we don't know if it's actually an error until we inspect the elements.
130	        val leftReified: Option[Vec[Data @unchecked]] =
131	          if (isView(left_v)) collectNotReadOnly(reifyIdentityView(left_v)) else Some(left_v)
132	        val rightReified: Option[Vec[Data @unchecked]] =
133	          if (isView(right_v)) collectNotReadOnly(reifyIdentityView(right_v)) else Some(right_v)
134	
135	        if (
136	          leftReified.nonEmpty && rightReified.nonEmpty && canFirrtlConnectData(
137	            leftReified.get,
138	            rightReified.get,
139	            sourceInfo,
140	            context_mod
141	          )
142	        ) {
143	          pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, leftReified.get.lref(sourceInfo), rightReified.get.lref(sourceInfo)))
144	        } else {
145	          for (idx <- 0 until left_v.length) {
146	            try {
147	              connect(sourceInfo, left_v(idx), right_v(idx), context_mod)
148	            } catch {
149	              case BiConnectException(message) => throw BiConnectException(s"($idx)$message")
150	            }
151	          }
152	        }
153	      }
154	      // Handle Vec connected to DontCare
155	      case (left_v: Vec[Data @unchecked], DontCare) => {
156	        for (idx <- 0 until left_v.length) {
157	          try {
158	            connect(sourceInfo, left_v(idx), right, context_mod)
159	          } catch {
160	            case BiConnectException(message) => throw BiConnectException(s"($idx)$message")
161	          }
162	        }
163	      }
164	      // Handle DontCare connected to Vec
165	      case (DontCare, right_v: Vec[Data @unchecked]) => {
166	        for (idx <- 0 until right_v.length) {
167	          try {
168	            connect(sourceInfo, left, right_v(idx), context_mod)
169	          } catch {
170	            case BiConnectException(message) => throw BiConnectException(s"($idx)$message")
171	          }
172	        }
173	      }
174	      // Handle Records defined in Chisel._ code by emitting a FIRRTL bulk
175	      // connect when possible and a partial connect otherwise
176	      case pair @ (left_r: Record, right_r: Record) =>
177	        // chisel3 <> is commutative but FIRRTL <- is not
178	        val flipConnection =
179	          !MonoConnect.canBeSink(left_r, context_mod) || !MonoConnect.canBeSource(right_r, context_mod)
180	        val (newLeft, newRight) = if (flipConnection) (right_r, left_r) else (left_r, right_r)
181	
182	        // Filter out read-only because we don't know if it's actually an error until we inspect the elements.
183	        val leftReified: Option[Record] =
184	          if (isView(newLeft)) collectNotReadOnly(reifyIdentityView(newLeft)) else Some(newLeft)
185	        val rightReified: Option[Record] =
186	          if (isView(newRight)) collectNotReadOnly(reifyIdentityView(newRight)) else Some(newRight)
187	
188	        if (
189	          leftReified.nonEmpty && rightReified.nonEmpty && canFirrtlConnectData(
190	            leftReified.get,
191	            rightReified.get,
192	            sourceInfo,
193	            context_mod
194	          )
195	        ) {
196	          pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, leftReified.get.lref(sourceInfo), rightReified.get.lref(sourceInfo)))
197	        } else {
198	          recordConnect(sourceInfo, left_r, right_r, context_mod)
199	        }
200	
201	      // Handle Records connected to DontCare
202	      case (left_r: Record, DontCare) =>
203	        // For each field in left, descend with right
204	        for ((field, left_sub) <- left_r._elements) {
205	          try {
206	            connect(sourceInfo, left_sub, right, context_mod)
207	          } catch {
208	            case BiConnectException(message) => throw BiConnectException(s".$field$message")
209	          }
210	        }
211	      case (DontCare, right_r: Record) =>
212	        // For each field in left, descend with right
213	        for ((field, right_sub) <- right_r._elements) {
214	          try {
215	            connect(sourceInfo, left, right_sub, context_mod)
216	          } catch {
217	            case BiConnectException(message) => throw BiConnectException(s".$field$message")
218	          }
219	        }
220	
221	      // Left and right are different subtypes of Data so fail
222	      case (left, right) => throw MismatchedException(left.toString, right.toString)
223	    }
224	  }
225	
226	  // Do connection of two Records
227	  def recordConnect(
228	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
229	    left_r:      Record,
230	    right_r:     Record,
231	    context_mod: RawModule
232	  ): Unit = {
233	    // Verify right has no extra fields that left doesn't have
234	
235	    // For each field in left, descend with right.
236	    // Don't bother doing this check if we don't expect it to necessarily pass.
237	    for ((field, right_sub) <- right_r._elements) {
238	      if (!left_r._elements.isDefinedAt(field)) {
239	        throw MissingLeftFieldException(field)
240	      }
241	    }
242	    // For each field in left, descend with right
243	    for ((field, left_sub) <- left_r._elements) {
244	      try {
245	        right_r._elements.get(field) match {
246	          case Some(right_sub) => connect(sourceInfo, left_sub, right_sub, context_mod)
247	          case None            => throw MissingRightFieldException(field)
248	        }
249	      } catch {
250	        case BiConnectException(message) => throw BiConnectException(s".$field$message")
251	      }
252	    }
253	  }
254	
255	  /** Check whether two Data can be bulk connected (<=) in FIRRTL. From the
256	    * FIRRTL specification, the following must hold for FIRRTL connection:
257	    *
258	    *   1. The Chisel types of the left-hand and right-hand side expressions must be
259	    *       equivalent (and thus will become the same FIRRTL type).
260	    *   2. The bit widths of the two expressions must allow for data to always
261	    *        flow from a smaller bit width to an equal size or larger bit width.
262	    *   3. The flow of the left-hand side expression must be sink or duplex
263	    *   4. Either the flow of the right-hand side expression is source or duplex,
264	    *      or the right-hand side expression has a passive type.
265	    *   5. No analog connections
266	    *
267	    * @param raiseIfFalse raise a BiConnectException if the result would be false with information about why it's false.
268	    */
269	  private[chisel3] def canFirrtlConnectData(
270	    sink:        Data,
271	    source:      Data,
272	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
273	    context_mod: BaseModule
274	  ): Boolean = {
275	
276	    // check that the aggregates have the same types
277	    def typeCheck = CheckTypes.validConnect(
278	      Converter.extractType(sink, sourceInfo),
279	      Converter.extractType(source, sourceInfo)
280	    )
281	
282	    // check records live in appropriate contexts
283	    def contextCheck =
284	      MonoConnect.dataConnectContextCheck(
285	        sourceInfo,
286	        sink,
287	        source,
288	        context_mod
289	      )
290	
291	    // sink must be writable and must also not be a literal
292	    def bindingCheck = sink.topBinding match {
293	      case _: ReadOnlyBinding => false
294	      case _ => true
295	    }
296	
297	    // check data can flow between provided aggregates
298	    def flowSinkCheck = MonoConnect.canBeSink(sink, context_mod)
299	    def flowSourceCheck = MonoConnect.canBeSource(source, context_mod)
300	
301	    // do not bulk connect source literals (results in infinite recursion from calling .ref)
302	    def sourceAndSinkNotLiteralOrViewCheck = List(source, sink).forall {
303	      _.topBinding match {
304	        case _: LitBinding           => false
305	        case _: ViewBinding          => false
306	        case _: AggregateViewBinding => false
307	        case _ => true
308	      }
309	    }
310	
311	    // do not bulk connect the 'io' pseudo-bundle of a BlackBox since it will be decomposed in FIRRTL
312	    def blackBoxCheck = Seq(source, sink).map(_._parent).forall {
313	      case Some(_: BlackBox @nowarn("cat=deprecation")) => false
314	      case _                                            => true
315	    }
316	
317	    // Do not bulk connect instance choices since they need to be expanded field-by-field.
318	    def instanceChoiceCheck = List(source, sink).forall {
319	      _.topBinding match {
320	        case _: InstanceChoiceBinding => false
321	        case _ => true
322	      }
323	    }
324	
325	    typeCheck && contextCheck && bindingCheck && flowSinkCheck && flowSourceCheck && sourceAndSinkNotLiteralOrViewCheck && blackBoxCheck && instanceChoiceCheck
326	  }
327	
328	  // These functions (finally) issue the connection operation
329	  // Issue with right as sink, left as source
330	  private def issueConnectL2R(
331	    left:    Element,
332	    leftWr:  ViewWriteability,
333	    right:   Element,
334	    rightWr: ViewWriteability
335	  )(
336	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
337	  ): Unit = {
338	    // Source and sink are ambiguous in the case of a Bi/Bulk Connect (<>).
339	    // If either is a DontCareBinding, just issue a DefInvalid for the other,
340	    //  otherwise, issue a Connect.
341	    (left.topBinding, right.topBinding) match {
342	      case (lb: DontCareBinding, _) =>
343	        rightWr.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
344	          pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, right.lref))
345	        }
346	      case (_, rb: DontCareBinding) =>
347	        leftWr.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
348	          pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, left.lref))
349	        }
350	      case (_, _) =>
351	        rightWr.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
352	          pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, right.lref, left.ref))
353	        }
354	    }
355	  }
356	  // Issue with left as sink, right as source
357	  private def issueConnectR2L(
358	    left:    Element,
359	    leftWr:  ViewWriteability,
360	    right:   Element,
361	    rightWr: ViewWriteability
362	  )(
363	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
364	  ): Unit = {
365	    // Source and sink are ambiguous in the case of a Bi/Bulk Connect (<>).
366	    // If either is a DontCareBinding, just issue a DefInvalid for the other,
367	    //  otherwise, issue a Connect.
368	    (left.topBinding, right.topBinding) match {
369	      case (lb: DontCareBinding, _) =>
370	        rightWr.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
371	          pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, right.lref))
372	        }
373	      case (_, rb: DontCareBinding) =>
374	        leftWr.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
375	          pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, left.lref))
376	        }
377	      case (_, _) =>
378	        leftWr.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
379	          pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, left.lref, right.ref))
380	        }
381	    }
382	  }
383	
384	  // This function checks if element-level connection operation allowed.
385	  // Then it either issues it or throws the appropriate exception.
386	  def elemConnect(
387	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo,
388	    _left:               Element,
389	    _right:              Element,
390	    context_mod:         RawModule
391	  ): Unit = {
392	    import BindingDirection.{Input, Internal, Output} // Using extensively so import these
393	    val (left, lwr) = reify(_left)
394	    val (right, rwr) = reify(_right)
395	    // If left or right have no location, assume in context module
396	    // This can occur if one of them is a literal, unbound will error previously
397	    val left_mod:  BaseModule = left.topBinding.location.getOrElse(context_mod)
398	    val right_mod: BaseModule = right.topBinding.location.getOrElse(context_mod)
399	
400	    val left_parent_opt = Builder.retrieveParent(left_mod, context_mod)
401	    val right_parent_opt = Builder.retrieveParent(right_mod, context_mod)
402	    val context_mod_opt = Some(context_mod)
403	
404	    val left_direction = BindingDirection.from(left.topBinding, left.direction)
405	    val right_direction = BindingDirection.from(right.topBinding, right.direction)
406	
407	    // CASE: Context is same module as left node and right node is in a child module
408	    if ((left_mod == context_mod) && (right_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
409	      // Thus, right node better be a port node and thus have a direction hint
410	      ((left_direction, right_direction): @unchecked) match {
411	        //    CURRENT MOD   CHILD MOD
412	        case (Input, Input)    => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
413	        case (Internal, Input) => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
414	
415	        case (Output, Output)   => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
416	        case (Internal, Output) => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
417	
418	        case (Input, Output) => throw BothDriversException
419	        case (Output, Input) => throw NeitherDriverException
420	        case (_, Internal)   => throw UnknownRelationException
421	      }
422	    }
423	
424	    // CASE: Context is same module as right node and left node is in child module
425	    else if ((right_mod == context_mod) && (left_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
426	      // Thus, left node better be a port node and thus have a direction hint
427	      ((left_direction, right_direction): @unchecked) match {
428	        //    CHILD MOD     CURRENT MOD
429	        case (Input, Input)    => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
430	        case (Input, Internal) => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
431	
432	        case (Output, Output)   => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
433	        case (Output, Internal) => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
434	
435	        case (Input, Output) => throw NeitherDriverException
436	        case (Output, Input) => throw BothDriversException
437	        case (Internal, _)   => throw UnknownRelationException
438	      }
439	    }
440	
441	    // CASE: Context is same module that both left node and right node are in
442	    else if ((context_mod == left_mod) && (context_mod == right_mod)) {
443	      ((left_direction, right_direction): @unchecked) match {
444	        //    CURRENT MOD   CURRENT MOD
445	        case (Input, Output)    => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
446	        case (Input, Internal)  => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
447	        case (Internal, Output) => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
448	
449	        case (Output, Input)    => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
450	        case (Output, Internal) => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
451	        case (Internal, Input)  => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
452	
453	        case (Input, Input)       => throw BothDriversException
454	        case (Output, Output)     => throw BothDriversException
455	        case (Internal, Internal) => throw UnknownDriverException
456	      }
457	    }
458	
459	    // CASE: Context is the parent module of both the module containing left node
460	    //                                        and the module containing right node
461	    //   Note: This includes case when left and right in same module but in parent
462	    else if ((left_parent_opt == context_mod_opt) && (right_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
463	      // Thus both nodes must be ports and have a direction hint
464	      ((left_direction, right_direction): @unchecked) match {
465	        //    CHILD MOD     CHILD MOD
466	        case (Input, Output) => issueConnectR2L(left, lwr, right, rwr)
467	        case (Output, Input) => issueConnectL2R(left, lwr, right, rwr)
468	
469	        case (Input, Input)   => throw NeitherDriverException
470	        case (Output, Output) => throw BothDriversException
471	        case (_, Internal)    => throw UnknownRelationException
472	        case (Internal, _)    => throw UnknownRelationException
473	      }
474	    }
475	
476	    // Not quite sure where left and right are compared to current module
477	    // so just error out
478	    else throw UnknownRelationException
479	  }
480	
481	  // This function checks if analog element-level attaching is allowed, then marks the Analog as connected
482	  def markAnalogConnected(sourceInfo: SourceInfo, analog: Analog, otherAnalog: Data, contextModule: RawModule): Unit = {
483	    analog.biConnectLocs.get(contextModule) match {
484	      case Some((si, data)) if data != otherAnalog =>
485	        throw AttachAlreadyBulkConnectedException(analog.toString, data.toString, otherAnalog.toString, si)
486	      case _ => // Do nothing
487	    }
488	    // Mark bulk connected
489	    analog.biConnectLocs(contextModule) = (sourceInfo, otherAnalog)
490	  }
491	}

18	/**
19	  * MonoConnect.connect executes a mono-directional connection element-wise.
20	  *
21	  * Note that this isn't commutative. There is an explicit source and sink
22	  * already determined before this function is called.
23	  *
24	  * The connect operation will recurse down the left Data (with the right Data).
25	  * An exception will be thrown if a movement through the left cannot be matched
26	  * in the right. The right side is allowed to have extra Record fields.
27	  * Vecs must still be exactly the same size.
28	  *
29	  * See elemConnect for details on how the root connections are issued.
30	  *
31	  * Note that a valid sink must be writable so, one of these must hold:
32	  * - Is an internal writable node (Reg or Wire)
33	  * - Is an output of the current module
34	  * - Is an input of a submodule of the current module
35	  *
36	  * Note that a valid source must be readable so, one of these must hold:
37	  * - Is an internal readable node (Reg, Wire, Op)
38	  * - Is a literal
39	  * - Is a port of the current module or submodule of the current module
40	  */
41	
42	private[chisel3] object MonoConnect {
43	  def formatName(data: Data) = s"""${data.earlyName} in ${data.parentNameOpt.getOrElse("(unknown)")}"""
44	
45	  // These are all the possible exceptions that can be thrown.
46	  // These are from element-level connection
47	  def UnreadableSourceException(sink: Data, source: Data) =
48	    MonoConnectException(
49	      s"""${formatName(source)} cannot be read from module ${sink.parentNameOpt.getOrElse("(unknown)")}."""
50	    )
51	  def UnwritableSinkException(sink: Data, source: Data) =
52	    MonoConnectException(
53	      s"""${formatName(sink)} cannot be written from module ${source.parentNameOpt.getOrElse("(unknown)")}."""
54	    )
55	  def escapedScopeErrorMsg(data: Data, blockInfo: SourceInfo) = {
56	    s"operand '$data' has escaped the scope of the block (${blockInfo.makeMessage()}) in which it was constructed."
57	  }
58	  def UnknownRelationException =
59	    MonoConnectException("Sink or source unavailable to current module.")
60	  // These are when recursing down aggregate types
61	  def MismatchedVecException =
62	    MonoConnectException("Sink and Source are different length Vecs.")
63	  def MissingFieldException(field: String) =
64	    MonoConnectException(s"Source Record missing field ($field).")
65	  def MismatchedException(sink: Data, source: Data) =
66	    MonoConnectException(
67	      s"Sink (${sink.cloneType.toString}) and Source (${source.cloneType.toString}) have different types."
68	    )
69	  def DontCareCantBeSink =
70	    MonoConnectException("DontCare cannot be a connection sink")
71	  def AnalogCantBeMonoSink(sink: Data) =
72	    MonoConnectException(s"Sink ${formatName(sink)} of type Analog cannot participate in a mono connection (:=)")
73	  def AnalogCantBeMonoSource(source: Data) =
74	    MonoConnectException(s"Source ${formatName(source)} of type Analog cannot participate in a mono connection (:=)")
75	  def AnalogMonoConnectionException(source: Data, sink: Data) =
76	    MonoConnectException(
77	      s"Source ${formatName(source)} and sink ${formatName(sink)} of type Analog cannot participate in a mono connection (:=)"
78	    )
79	  def SourceProbeMonoConnectionException(source: Data) =
80	    MonoConnectException(s"Source ${formatName(source)} of Probed type cannot participate in a mono connection (:=)")
81	  def SinkProbeMonoConnectionException(sink: Data) =
82	    MonoConnectException(s"Sink ${formatName(sink)} of Probed type cannot participate in a mono connection (:=)")
83	
84	  /** Check if the argument is visible from current block scope
85	    *
86	    * Returns source locator of original block declaration if not visible (for error reporting)
87	    *
88	    * @return None if visible, Some(location of original block declaration)
89	    */
90	  def checkBlockVisibility(x: Data): Option[SourceInfo] = {
91	    require(Builder.hasDynamicContext, "block visibility cannot currently be determined without context")
92	    // TODO: Checking block stack isn't quite right in situations where the stack of blocks
93	    // doesn't reflect the generated structure (for example using withRegion to jump multiple levels).
94	    def visible(b: Block) = Builder.blockStack.contains(b)
95	    x.topBinding match {
96	      case mp: MemoryPortBinding =>
97	        None // TODO (albert-magyar): remove this "bridge" for odd enable logic of current CHIRRTL memories
98	      case DynamicIndexBinding(vec) => checkBlockVisibility(vec) // This goes with the MemoryPortBinding hack
99	      case bb: BlockBinding => bb.parentBlock.collect { case b: Block if !visible(b) => b.sourceInfo }
100	      case _ => None
101	    }
102	  }
103	
104	  private[chisel3] def reportIfReadOnly[T <: Data](x: (T, ViewWriteability))(implicit info: SourceInfo): T = {
105	    val (data, writable) = x
106	    writable.reportIfReadOnly(data)(WireInit(data))
107	  }
108	
109	  /** This function is what recursively tries to connect a sink and source together
110	    *
111	    * There is some cleverness in the use of internal try-catch to catch exceptions
112	    * during the recursive decent and then rethrow them with extra information added.
113	    * This gives the user a 'path' to where in the connections things went wrong.
114	    */
115	  def connect(
116	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
117	    sink:        Data,
118	    source:      Data,
119	    context_mod: BaseModule
120	  ): Unit = {
121	    (sink, source) match {
122	      // Two probes are connected at the root.
123	      case (sink_e, source_e)
124	          if (DataMirror.hasProbeTypeModifier(sink_e) && DataMirror.hasProbeTypeModifier(source_e)) =>
125	        probeDefine(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
126	
127	      // A probe-y thing cannot be connected to a different probe-y thing.
128	      case (_, source_e: Data) if DataMirror.hasProbeTypeModifier(source_e) =>
129	        throw SourceProbeMonoConnectionException(source_e)
130	      case (sink_e: Data, _) if DataMirror.hasProbeTypeModifier(sink_e) =>
131	        throw SinkProbeMonoConnectionException(sink_e)
132	
133	      // Handle legal element cases, note (Bool, Bool) is caught by the first two, as Bool is a UInt
134	      case (sink_e: Bool, source_e: UInt) =>
135	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
136	      case (sink_e: UInt, source_e: Bool) =>
137	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
138	      case (sink_e: UInt, source_e: UInt) =>
139	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
140	      case (sink_e: SInt, source_e: SInt) =>
141	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
142	      case (sink_e: Clock, source_e: Clock) =>
143	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
144	      case (sink_e: AsyncReset, source_e: AsyncReset) =>
145	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
146	      case (sink_e: ResetType, source_e: Reset) =>
147	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
148	      case (sink_e: Reset, source_e: ResetType) =>
149	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
150	      case (sink_e: EnumType, source_e: UnsafeEnum) =>
151	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
152	      case (sink_e: EnumType, source_e: EnumType) if sink_e.typeEquivalent(source_e) =>
153	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
154	      case (sink_e: UnsafeEnum, source_e: UInt) =>
155	        elemConnect(sourceInfo, sink_e, source_e, context_mod)
156	      case (sink_p: Property[_], source_p: Property[_]) =>
157	        propConnect(sourceInfo, sink_p, source_p, context_mod)
158	
159	      // Handle Vec case
160	      case (sink_v: Vec[Data @unchecked], source_v: Vec[Data @unchecked]) =>
161	        if (sink_v.length != source_v.length) { throw MismatchedVecException }
162	
163	        val sinkReified: Option[Vec[Data @unchecked]] =
164	          if (isView(sink_v)) reifyIdentityView(sink_v).map(reportIfReadOnly(_)(sourceInfo)) else Some(sink_v)
165	        val sourceReified: Option[Vec[Data @unchecked]] =
166	          if (isView(source_v)) reifyIdentityView(source_v).map(_._1) else Some(source_v)
167	
168	        if (
169	          sinkReified.nonEmpty && sourceReified.nonEmpty && canFirrtlConnectData(
170	            sinkReified.get,
171	            sourceReified.get,
172	            sourceInfo,
173	            context_mod
174	          )
175	        ) {
176	          pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, sinkReified.get.lref(sourceInfo), sourceReified.get.ref(sourceInfo)))
177	        } else {
178	          for (idx <- 0 until sink_v.length) {
179	            try {
180	              connect(sourceInfo, sink_v(idx), source_v(idx), context_mod)
181	            } catch {
182	              case MonoConnectException(message) => throw MonoConnectException(s"($idx)$message")
183	            }
184	          }
185	        }
186	      // Handle Vec connected to DontCare. Apply the DontCare to individual elements.
187	      case (sink_v: Vec[Data @unchecked], DontCare) =>
188	        for (idx <- 0 until sink_v.length) {
189	          try {
190	            connect(sourceInfo, sink_v(idx), source, context_mod)
191	          } catch {
192	            case MonoConnectException(message) => throw MonoConnectException(s"($idx)$message")
193	          }
194	        }
195	
196	      // Handle Record case
197	      case (sink_r: Record, source_r: Record) =>
198	        val sinkReified: Option[Record] =
199	          if (isView(sink_r)) reifyIdentityView(sink_r).map(reportIfReadOnly(_)(sourceInfo)) else Some(sink_r)
200	        val sourceReified: Option[Record] =
201	          if (isView(source_r)) reifyIdentityView(source_r).map(_._1) else Some(source_r)
202	
203	        if (
204	          sinkReified.nonEmpty && sourceReified.nonEmpty && canFirrtlConnectData(
205	            sinkReified.get,
206	            sourceReified.get,
207	            sourceInfo,
208	            context_mod
209	          )
210	        ) {
211	          pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, sinkReified.get.lref(sourceInfo), sourceReified.get.ref(sourceInfo)))
212	        } else {
213	          // For each field, descend with right
214	          for ((field, sink_sub) <- sink_r._elements) {
215	            try {
216	              source_r._elements.get(field) match {
217	                case Some(source_sub) => connect(sourceInfo, sink_sub, source_sub, context_mod)
218	                case None             => throw MissingFieldException(field)
219	              }
220	            } catch {
221	              case MonoConnectException(message) => throw MonoConnectException(s".$field$message")
222	            }
223	          }
224	        }
225	      // Handle Record connected to DontCare. Apply the DontCare to individual elements.
226	      case (sink_r: Record, DontCare) =>
227	        // For each field, descend with right
228	        for ((field, sink_sub) <- sink_r._elements) {
229	          try {
230	            connect(sourceInfo, sink_sub, source, context_mod)
231	          } catch {
232	            case MonoConnectException(message) => throw MonoConnectException(s".$field$message")
233	          }
234	        }
235	
236	      // Source is DontCare - it may be connected to anything. It generates a defInvalid for the sink.
237	      case (_sink: Element, DontCare) =>
238	        val (sink, writable) = reify(_sink) // Handle views.
239	        writable.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
240	          pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, sink.lref(sourceInfo))): Unit
241	        }(sourceInfo) // Nothing to push if an error.
242	
243	      // DontCare as a sink is illegal.
244	      case (DontCare, _) => throw DontCareCantBeSink
245	      // Analog is illegal in mono connections.
246	      case (_: Analog, _: Analog) => throw AnalogMonoConnectionException(source, sink)
247	      // Analog is illegal in mono connections.
248	      case (_: Analog, _) => throw AnalogCantBeMonoSink(sink)
249	      // Analog is illegal in mono connections.
250	      case (_, _: Analog) => throw AnalogCantBeMonoSource(source)
251	      // Sink and source are different subtypes of data so fail
252	      case (sink, source) => throw MismatchedException(sink, source)
253	    }
254	  }
255	
256	  /** Determine if a valid connection can be made between a source [[Data]] and sink
257	    * [[Data]] given their parent module and directionality context
258	    *
259	    * @return whether the source and sink exist in an appropriate context to be connected
260	    */
261	  private[chisel3] def dataConnectContextCheck(
262	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo,
263	    sink:                Data,
264	    source:              Data,
265	    context_mod:         BaseModule
266	  ): Boolean = {
267	    import ActualDirection.{Bidirectional, Input, Output}
268	    // If source has no location, assume in context module
269	    // This can occur if is a literal, unbound will error previously
270	    val sink_mod:   BaseModule = sink.topBinding.location.getOrElse(throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source))
271	    val source_mod: BaseModule = source.topBinding.location.getOrElse(context_mod)
272	
273	    val sink_parent_opt = Builder.retrieveParent(sink_mod, context_mod)
274	    val source_parent_opt = Builder.retrieveParent(source_mod, context_mod)
275	    val context_mod_opt = Some(context_mod)
276	
277	    val sink_is_port = sink.topBinding match {
278	      case PortBinding(_) => true
279	      case _              => false
280	    }
281	    val source_is_port = source.topBinding match {
282	      case PortBinding(_) => true
283	      case _              => false
284	    }
285	
286	    checkBlockVisibility(sink) match {
287	      case Some(blockInfo) => Builder.error(escapedScopeErrorMsg(sink, blockInfo))
288	      case None            => ()
289	    }
290	
291	    checkBlockVisibility(source) match {
292	      case Some(blockInfo) => Builder.error(escapedScopeErrorMsg(source, blockInfo))
293	      case None            => ()
294	    }
295	
296	    // CASE: Context is same module that both sink node and source node are in
297	    if ((context_mod == sink_mod) && (context_mod == source_mod)) {
298	      !sink_is_port || sink.direction != Input
299	    }
300	
301	    // CASE: Context is same module as sink node and source node is in a child module
302	    else if ((sink_mod == context_mod) && (source_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
303	      // NOTE: Workaround for bulk connecting non-agnostified FIRRTL ports
304	      // See: https://github.com/freechipsproject/firrtl/issues/1703
305	      // Original behavior should just check if the sink direction is an Input
306	      val sinkCanBeInput = sink.direction match {
307	        case Input => true
308	        case _: Bidirectional => true
309	        case _ => false
310	      }
311	      // Thus, right node better be a port node and thus have a direction
312	      if (!source_is_port) { false }
313	      else if (sinkCanBeInput) {
314	        if (source.direction == Output) { true }
315	        else { false }
316	      } else { true }
317	    }
318	
319	    // CASE: Context is same module as source node and sink node is in child module
320	    else if ((source_mod == context_mod) && (sink_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
321	      // NOTE: Workaround for bulk connecting non-agnostified FIRRTL ports
322	      // See: https://github.com/freechipsproject/firrtl/issues/1703
323	      // Original behavior should just check if the sink direction is an Input
324	      sink.direction match {
325	        case Input => true
326	        case _: Bidirectional => true
327	        case _ => false
328	      }
329	    }
330	
331	    // CASE: Context is the parent module of both the module containing sink node
332	    //                                        and the module containing source node
333	    //   Note: This includes case when sink and source in same module but in parent
334	    else if ((sink_parent_opt == context_mod_opt) && (source_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
335	      // Thus both nodes must be ports and have a direction
336	      if (!source_is_port) { false }
337	      else if (sink_is_port) {
338	        // NOTE: Workaround for bulk connecting non-agnostified FIRRTL ports
339	        // See: https://github.com/freechipsproject/firrtl/issues/1703
340	        // Original behavior should just check if the sink direction is an Input
341	        sink.direction match {
342	          case Input => true
343	          case _: Bidirectional => true // NOTE: Workaround for non-agnostified ports
344	          case _ => false
345	        }
346	      } else { false }
347	    }
348	
349	    // Not quite sure where left and right are compared to current module
350	    // so just error out
351	    else false
352	  }
353	
354	  /** Trace flow from child Data to its parent.
355	    *
356	    * Returns true if, given the context,
357	    * this signal can be a sink when wantsToBeSink = true,
358	    * or if it can be a source when wantsToBeSink = false.
359	    * Always returns true if the Data does not actually correspond
360	    * to a Port.
361	    */
362	  @tailrec private[chisel3] def traceFlow(
363	    wantToBeSink:     Boolean,
364	    currentlyFlipped: Boolean,
365	    data:             Data,
366	    context_mod:      BaseModule
367	  ): Boolean = {
368	    val sdir = data.specifiedDirection
369	    val coercedFlip = sdir == SpecifiedDirection.Input
370	    val coercedAlign = sdir == SpecifiedDirection.Output
371	    val flipped = sdir == SpecifiedDirection.Flip
372	    val traceFlipped = ((flipped ^ currentlyFlipped) || coercedFlip) && (!coercedAlign)
373	    data.binding.get match {
374	      case ChildBinding(parent) => traceFlow(wantToBeSink, traceFlipped, parent, context_mod)
375	      case PortBinding(enclosure) =>
376	        val childPort = enclosure != context_mod
377	        wantToBeSink ^ childPort ^ traceFlipped
378	      case _ => true
379	    }
380	  }
381	  def canBeSink(data:   Data, context_mod: BaseModule): Boolean = traceFlow(true, false, data, context_mod)
382	  def canBeSource(data: Data, context_mod: BaseModule): Boolean = traceFlow(false, false, data, context_mod)
383	
384	  /** Check whether two Data can be bulk connected (<=) in FIRRTL. (MonoConnect case)
385	    *
386	    * Mono-directional bulk connects only work if all signals of the sink are unidirectional
387	    * In the case of a sink aggregate with bidirectional signals, e.g. `Decoupled`,
388	    * a `BiConnect` (`chisel3.<>` or `chisel.:<>=`) is necessary.
389	    */
390	  private[chisel3] def canFirrtlConnectData(
391	    sink:        Data,
392	    source:      Data,
393	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
394	    context_mod: BaseModule
395	  ): Boolean = {
396	    // Assuming we're using a <>, check if a FIRRTL.<= connection operator is valid in that case
397	    def biConnectCheck =
398	      BiConnect.canFirrtlConnectData(sink, source, sourceInfo, context_mod)
399	
400	    // Check that the sink Data can be driven (not bidirectional or an input) to match Chisel semantics
401	    def sinkCanBeDrivenCheck: Boolean =
402	      sink.direction == ActualDirection.Output || sink.direction == ActualDirection.Unspecified
403	
404	    biConnectCheck && sinkCanBeDrivenCheck
405	  }
406	
407	  // This function (finally) issues the connection operation
408	  private def issueConnect(sink: Element, source: Element)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit = {
409	    // If the source is a DontCare, generate a DefInvalid for the sink,
410	    //  otherwise, issue a Connect.
411	    source.topBinding match {
412	      case b: DontCareBinding => pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, sink.lref))
413	      case _ => pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, sink.lref, source.ref))
414	    }
415	  }
416	
417	  // This function checks if element-level connection operation allowed.
418	  // Then it either issues it or throws the appropriate exception.
419	  def elemConnect(
420	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo,
421	    _sink:               Element,
422	    _source:             Element,
423	    context_mod:         BaseModule
424	  ): Unit = {
425	    // Reify sink and source if they're views.
426	    val (sink, writable) = reify(_sink)
427	    val (source, _) = reify(_source)
428	
429	    checkConnect(sourceInfo, sink, source, context_mod)
430	    writable.reportIfReadOnlyUnit(issueConnect(sink, source))
431	  }
432	
433	  def propConnect(
434	    sourceInfo: SourceInfo,
435	    sinkProp:   Property[_],
436	    sourceProp: Property[_],
437	    context:    BaseModule
438	  ): Unit = {
439	    implicit val info: SourceInfo = sourceInfo
440	    // Reify sink and source if they're views.
441	    val (sink, writable) = reify(sinkProp)
442	    val (source, _) = reify(sourceProp)
443	
444	    checkConnect(sourceInfo, sink, source, context)
445	    // Add the PropAssign command directly onto the correct BaseModule subclass.
446	    context match {
447	      case rm: RawModule =>
448	        writable.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
449	          rm.addCommand(PropAssign(sourceInfo, sink.lref, source.ref))
450	        }(sourceInfo)
451	      case cls: Class =>
452	        writable.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
453	          cls.addCommand(PropAssign(sourceInfo, sink.lref, source.ref))
454	        }(sourceInfo)
455	      case _ => throwException("Internal Error! Property connection can only occur within RawModule or Class.")
456	    }
457	  }
458	
459	  def probeDefine(
460	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
461	    sinkProbe:   Data,
462	    sourceProbe: Data,
463	    context:     BaseModule
464	  ): Unit = {
465	    implicit val info: SourceInfo = sourceInfo
466	    val (sink, writable) = reifyIdentityView(sinkProbe).getOrElse(
467	      throwException(
468	        s"If a DataView contains a Probe, it must resolve to one Data. $sinkProbe does not meet this criteria."
469	      )
470	    )
471	    val (source, _) = reifyIdentityView(sourceProbe).getOrElse(
472	      throwException(
473	        s"If a DataView contains a Probe, it must resolve to one Data. $sourceProbe does not meet this criteria."
474	      )
475	    )
476	    checkConnect.checkConnection(sourceInfo, sink, source, context)
477	    context match {
478	      case rm: RawModule =>
479	        writable.reportIfReadOnlyUnit {
480	          rm.addCommand(ProbeDefine(sourceInfo, sink.lref, source.ref))
481	        }(sourceInfo) // Nothing to push if an error.
482	      case _ => throwException("Internal Error! Probe connection can only occur within RawModule.")
483	    }
484	  }
485	}
