487	/** This object can be applied to check if element-level connection is allowed.
488	  *
489	  * Its apply methods throw the appropriate exception, if necessary.
490	  */
491	private[chisel3] object checkConnect {
492	  def apply(
493	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
494	    sink:        Element,
495	    source:      Element,
496	    context_mod: BaseModule
497	  ): Unit = {
498	    checkConnection(sourceInfo, sink, source, context_mod)
499	
500	    // Extra checks for Property[_] elements.
501	    (sink, source) match {
502	      case (sinkProp: Property[_], sourceProp: Property[_]) =>
503	        checkConnect.checkPropertyConnection(sourceInfo, sinkProp, sourceProp)
504	      case (_, _) =>
505	    }
506	  }
507	
508	  def checkConnection(
509	    sourceInfo:  SourceInfo,
510	    sink:        Data,
511	    source:      Data,
512	    context_mod: BaseModule
513	  ): Unit = {
514	    import BindingDirection.{Input, Internal, Output} // Using extensively so import these
515	
516	    // Import helpers and exception types.
517	    import MonoConnect.{
518	      checkBlockVisibility,
519	      escapedScopeErrorMsg,
520	      UnknownRelationException,
521	      UnreadableSourceException,
522	      UnwritableSinkException
523	    }
524	
525	    // If source has no location, assume in context module
526	    // This can occur if is a literal, unbound will error previously
527	    val sink_mod:   BaseModule = sink.topBinding.location.getOrElse(throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source))
528	    val source_mod: BaseModule = source.topBinding.location.getOrElse(context_mod)
529	
530	    val sink_parent_opt = Builder.retrieveParent(sink_mod, context_mod)
531	    val source_parent_opt = Builder.retrieveParent(source_mod, context_mod)
532	    val context_mod_opt = Some(context_mod)
533	
534	    val sink_direction = BindingDirection.from(sink.topBinding, sink.direction)
535	    val source_direction = BindingDirection.from(source.topBinding, source.direction)
536	
537	    // CASE: Context is same module that both left node and right node are in
538	    if ((context_mod == sink_mod) && (context_mod == source_mod)) {
539	      ((sink_direction, source_direction): @unchecked) match {
540	        //    SINK          SOURCE
541	        //    CURRENT MOD   CURRENT MOD
542	        case (Output, _)   => ()
543	        case (Internal, _) => ()
544	        case (Input, _)    => throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source)
545	      }
546	    }
547	
548	    // CASE: Context is same module as sink node and right node is in a child module
549	    else if ((sink_mod == context_mod) && (source_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
550	      // Thus, right node better be a port node and thus have a direction
551	      ((sink_direction, source_direction): @unchecked) match {
552	        //    SINK          SOURCE
553	        //    CURRENT MOD   CHILD MOD
554	        case (Internal, Output) => ()
555	        case (Internal, Input)  => ()
556	        case (Output, Output)   => ()
557	        case (Output, Input)    => ()
558	        case (_, Internal)      => throw UnreadableSourceException(sink, source)
559	        case (Input, Output)    => ()
560	        case (Input, _)         => throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source)
561	      }
562	    }
563	
564	    // CASE: Context is same module as source node and sink node is in child module
565	    else if ((source_mod == context_mod) && (sink_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
566	      // Thus, left node better be a port node and thus have a direction
567	      ((sink_direction, source_direction): @unchecked) match {
568	        //    SINK          SOURCE
569	        //    CHILD MOD     CURRENT MOD
570	        case (Input, _)    => ()
571	        case (Output, _)   => throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source)
572	        case (Internal, _) => throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source)
573	      }
574	    }
575	
576	    // CASE: Context is the parent module of both the module containing sink node
577	    //                                        and the module containing source node
578	    //   Note: This includes case when sink and source in same module but in parent
579	    else if ((sink_parent_opt == context_mod_opt) && (source_parent_opt == context_mod_opt)) {
580	      // Thus both nodes must be ports and have a direction
581	      ((sink_direction, source_direction): @unchecked) match {
582	        //    SINK          SOURCE
583	        //    CHILD MOD     CHILD MOD
584	        case (Input, Input)  => ()
585	        case (Input, Output) => ()
586	        case (Output, _)     => throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source)
587	        case (_, Internal)   => throw UnreadableSourceException(sink, source)
588	        case (Internal, _)   => throw UnwritableSinkException(sink, source)
589	      }
590	    }
591	
592	    // Not quite sure where left and right are compared to current module
593	    // so just error out
594	    else throw UnknownRelationException
595	
596	    checkBlockVisibility(sink) match {
597	      case Some(blockInfo) => Builder.error(escapedScopeErrorMsg(sink, blockInfo))(sourceInfo)
598	      case None            => ()
599	    }
600	
601	    checkBlockVisibility(source) match {
602	      case Some(blockInfo) => Builder.error(escapedScopeErrorMsg(source, blockInfo))(sourceInfo)
603	      case None            => ()
604	    }
605	  }
606	
607	  // Extra checks specific to Property types. Most of the checks are baked into the Scala type system, but the actual
608	  // class names underlying a Property[ClassType] are runtime values, so the check that they match is a runtime check.
609	  def checkPropertyConnection(sourceInfo: SourceInfo, sink: Property[_], source: Property[_]): Unit = {
610	    // If source is a ClassType, get the source Property[ClassType] expected class name.
611	    val sourceClassNameOpt = source.getPropertyType match {
612	      case ClassPropertyType(name) => Some(name)
613	      case _                       => None
614	    }
615	
616	    // If sink is a ClassType, get the sink Property[ClassType] expected class name.
617	    val sinkClassNameOpt = sink.getPropertyType match {
618	      case ClassPropertyType(name) => Some(name)
619	      case _                       => None
620	    }
621	
622	    // We are guaranteed that source and sink are the same Property type by the Scala type system, but if they're both
623	    // Property[ClassType] we need to check they are the same class.
624	    (sourceClassNameOpt, sinkClassNameOpt) match {
625	      case (Some(sourceClassName), Some(sinkClassName)) if (sinkClassName != sourceClassName) =>
626	        throwException(
627	          sourceInfo.makeMessage(info =>
628	            s"Sink Property[ClassType] expected class $sinkClassName, but source Instance[Class] was class $sourceClassName $info"
629	          )
630	        )
631	      case (_, _) => ()
632	    }
633	  }
634	}

22	class AliasedAggregateFieldException(message: String) extends chisel3.ChiselException(message)

24	/** An abstract class for data types that solely consist of (are an aggregate
25	  * of) other Data objects.
26	  */
27	sealed trait Aggregate extends Data {
28	
29	  protected def checkingLitOption(checkForDontCares: Boolean): Option[BigInt] = {
30	    // Shift the accumulated value by our width and add in our component, masked by our width.
31	    def shiftAdd(elt: Data, accumulator: Option[BigInt]): Option[BigInt] = {
32	      (accumulator, elt.litOption) match {
33	        case (Some(accumulator), Some(eltLit)) =>
34	          val width = elt.width.get
35	          val masked = ((BigInt(1) << width) - 1) & eltLit // also handles the negative case with two's complement
36	          Some((accumulator << width) + masked)
37	        case (Some(accumulator), None) if checkForDontCares =>
38	          Builder.error(s"Called litValue on aggregate $this contains DontCare")(UnlocatableSourceInfo)
39	          None
40	        case (None, _) => None
41	        case (_, None) => None
42	      }
43	    }
44	
45	    topBindingOpt match {
46	      // Don't accidentally invent a literal value for a view that is empty
47	      case Some(_: AggregateViewBinding) if this.getElements.isEmpty =>
48	        reifyIdentityView(this) match {
49	          case Some((target: Aggregate, _)) => target.checkingLitOption(checkForDontCares)
50	          // This can occur with empty Vecs or Bundles
51	          case _ => None
52	        }
53	      case Some(_: BundleLitBinding | _: VecLitBinding | _: AggregateViewBinding) =>
54	        // Records store elements in reverse order and higher indices are more significant in Vecs
55	        this.getElements.foldRight(Option(BigInt(0)))(shiftAdd)
56	      case _ => None
57	    }
58	  }
59	
60	  /** Return an Aggregate's literal value if it is a literal, None otherwise.
61	    * If any element of the aggregate is not a literal (or DontCare), the result isn't a literal.
62	    *
63	    * @note [[DontCare]] is allowed and will be replaced with 0. Use [[litValue]] to disallow DontCare.
64	    * @return an Aggregate's literal value if it is a literal, None otherwise.
65	    */
66	  override def litOption: Option[BigInt] = {
67	    checkingLitOption(checkForDontCares = false)
68	  }
69	
70	  /** Return an Aggregate's literal value if it is a literal, otherwise an exception is thrown.
71	    * If any element of the aggregate is not a literal with a defined width, the result isn't a literal.
72	    *
73	    * @return an Aggregate's literal value if it is a literal, exception otherwise.
74	    */
75	  override def litValue: BigInt = {
76	    checkingLitOption(checkForDontCares = true).getOrElse(
77	      throw new ChiselException(s"Cannot ask for litValue of $this as it is not a literal.")
78	    )
79	  }
80	
81	  /** Returns a Seq of the immediate contents of this Aggregate, in order.
82	    */
83	  def getElements: Seq[Data]
84	
85	  /** Save this result, as it can be very expensive to determine this otherwise */
86	  private[chisel3] lazy val elementsContainProbe: Boolean =
87	    elementsIterator.exists(d => chisel3.internal.containsProbe(d))
88	
89	  /** Similar to [[getElements]] but allows for more optimized use */
90	  private[chisel3] def elementsIterator: Iterator[Data]
91	
92	  private[chisel3] def width: Width = elementsIterator.map(_.width).foldLeft(0.W)(_ + _)
93	
94	  // Emits the FIRRTL `this <= that`, or `this is invalid` if that == DontCare
95	  private[chisel3] def firrtlConnect(that: Data)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit = {
96	    // If the source is a DontCare, generate a DefInvalid for the sink, otherwise, issue a Connect.
97	    if (that == DontCare) {
98	      pushCommand(DefInvalid(sourceInfo, lref))
99	    } else {
100	      pushCommand(Connect(sourceInfo, lref, Node(that)))
101	    }
102	  }
103	
104	  // Due to prior lack of zero-width wire support, .asUInt for an empty Aggregate has returned 0.U (equivalent to 0.U(1.W))
105	  // In the case where an empty Aggregate is a child of an outer Aggregate, however, it would flatten out the empty inner Aggregate
106	  // This means we need the `first` argument so that we can preserve this behavior of Aggregates while still allowing subclasses
107	  // to override .asUInt behavior
108	  override private[chisel3] def _asUIntImpl(first: Boolean)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): UInt = {
109	    checkingLitOption(checkForDontCares = false) match {
110	      case Some(value) =>
111	        // Using UInt.Lit instead of .U so we can use Width argument which may be Unknown
112	        UInt.Lit(value, this.width)
113	      case None =>
114	        val elts = this.getElements.map(_._asUIntImpl(false))
115	        if (elts.isEmpty && !first) 0.U(0.W) else SeqUtils.asUInt(elts)
116	    }
117	  }
118	
119	  // Return a literal of the same type from a Seq of literals for each leaf
120	  private[chisel3] final def _makeLitFromLeaves(elems: Seq[Element])(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Data = {
121	    // We could use virtual methods instead of matching on concrete subtypes,
122	    // but the difference for Record vs Vec is so small, it doesn't seem worth it
123	    val clone: Aggregate = this.cloneTypeFull
124	    val mapping = clone.flatten.view
125	      .zip(elems)
126	      .map { case (thisElt, litElt) =>
127	        val litArg = litElt.topBindingOpt match {
128	          case Some(ElementLitBinding(value)) => value
129	          case _ => throwException(s"Internal Error! For field $thisElt, given non-literal $litElt!")
130	        }
131	        thisElt -> litArg
132	      }
133	    val binding = clone match {
134	      case r: Record => BundleLitBinding(mapping.to(Map))
135	      case v: Vec[_] => VecLitBinding(mapping.to(VectorMap))
136	    }
137	    clone.bind(binding)
138	    clone
139	  }

141	  override protected def _fromUInt(that: UInt)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Data = {
142	    val _asUInt = _resizeToWidth(that, this.widthOption, true)(identity)
143	    // If that is a literal and all constituent Elements can be represented as literals, return a literal
144	    val ((_, allLit), rvalues) = {
145	      this.flatten.toList.mapAccumulate[(Int, Boolean), Element]((0, _asUInt.isLit)) { case ((lo, literal), elt) =>
146	        val hi = lo + elt.getWidth
147	        // Chisel only supports zero width extraction if hi = -1 and lo = 0, so do it manually
148	        val _extracted = if (elt.getWidth == 0) 0.U(0.W) else _asUInt(hi - 1, lo)
149	        // _fromUInt returns Data but we know that it is an Element
150	        val rhs = elt._fromUIntPrivate(_extracted).asInstanceOf[Element]
151	        ((hi, literal && rhs.isLit), rhs)
152	      }
153	    }
154	    if (allLit) {
155	      this._makeLitFromLeaves(rvalues)
156	    } else {
157	      val _wire = Wire(this.cloneTypeFull)
158	      for ((l, r) <- _wire.flatten.zip(rvalues)) {
159	        l := r
160	      }
161	      _wire
162	    }
163	  }
164	}

170	    * @note elements are NOT assigned by default and have no value
171	    */

177	  /** Truncate an index to implement modulo-power-of-2 addressing. */
178	  private[chisel3] def truncateIndex(
179	    idx: UInt,
180	    n:   BigInt
181	  )(
182	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
183	  ): UInt = {
184	    val w = (n - 1).bitLength
185	    if (n <= 1) WireInit(0.U) // Need the Wire otherwise we emit vec[0] which is illegal FIRRTL.
186	    // Other cases do not need a Wire because the literal is truncated to fit.
187	    else if (idx.width.known && idx.width.get <= w) idx
188	    else if (idx.width.known) idx(w - 1, 0)
189	    else (idx | 0.U(w.W))(w - 1, 0)
190	  }

193	/** A vector (array) of [[Data]] elements. Provides hardware versions of various
194	  * collection transformation functions found in software array implementations.
195	  *
196	  * Careful consideration should be given over the use of [[Vec]] vs
197	  * [[scala.collection.immutable.Seq Seq]] or some other Scala collection. In general [[Vec]] only
198	  * needs to be used when there is a need to express the hardware collection in a [[Reg]] or IO
199	  * [[Bundle]] or when access to elements of the array is indexed via a hardware signal.
200	  *
201	  * Example of indexing into a [[Vec]] using a hardware address and where the [[Vec]] is defined in
202	  * an IO [[Bundle]]
203	  *
204	  *  {{{
205	  *    val io = IO(new Bundle {
206	  *      val in = Input(Vec(20, UInt(16.W)))
207	  *      val addr = Input(UInt(5.W))
208	  *      val out = Output(UInt(16.W))
209	  *    })
210	  *    io.out := io.in(io.addr)
211	  *  }}}
212	  *
213	  * @tparam T type of elements
214	  *
215	  * @note
216	  *  - when multiple conflicting assignments are performed on a Vec element, the last one takes effect (unlike Mem, where the result is undefined)
217	  *  - Vecs, unlike classes in Scala's collection library, are propagated intact to FIRRTL as a vector type, which may make debugging easier
218	  */

221	    with VecIntf[T]
222	    with VecLike[T] {

227	        val contents = vecLitBinding.zipWithIndex.map { case ((data, lit), index) =>
228	          s"$index=$lit"
229	        }.mkString(", ")
230	        s"${sample_element.cloneType}[$length]($contents)"
231	      case _ => stringAccessor(s"${sample_element.cloneType}[$length]")

245	  override private[chisel3] def bind(target: Binding, parentDirection: SpecifiedDirection): Unit = {
246	    this.maybeAddToParentIds(target)

249	    val resolvedDirection = SpecifiedDirection.fromParent(parentDirection, specifiedDirection)

252	    val childBinding = ChildBinding(this)

289	  /** The "bulk connect operator", assigning elements in this Vec from elements in a Seq.
290	    *
291	    * For chisel3._, uses the `chisel3.internal.BiConnect` algorithm; sub-elements of `that` may end up driving sub-elements of `this`
292	    *  - Complicated semantics, will likely be deprecated in the future
293	    *
294	    * For Chisel._, emits the FIRRTL.<- operator
295	    *  - Equivalent to `this :<>= that` but bundle field names and vector sizes do not have to match
296	    *
297	    * @note the length of this Vec and that Seq must match
298	    * @param that the Seq to connect from
299	    * @group connection
300	    */

302	    if (this.length != that.length)
303	      Builder.error(
304	        s"Vec (size ${this.length}) and Seq (size ${that.length}) being bulk connected have different lengths!"
305	      )
306	    for ((a, b) <- this.zip(that)) {

311	  /** The "bulk connect operator", assigning elements in this Vec from elements in a Vec.
312	    *
313	    * For chisel3._, uses the `chisel3.internal.BiConnect` algorithm; sub-elements of `that` may end up driving sub-elements of `this`
314	    *  - See docs/src/explanations/connection-operators.md for details
315	    *
316	    * For Chisel._, emits the FIRRTL.<- operator
317	    *  - Equivalent to `this :<>= that` without the restrictions that bundle field names and vector sizes must match
318	    *
319	    * @note This is necessary in [[Aggregate]], rather than relying on [[Data.<>]], due to supporting the Seq
320	    * @note the length of this Vec and that Vec must match
321	    * @param that the Vec to connect from

324	  def <>(that: Vec[T])(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =

327	  /** "The strong connect operator", assigning elements in this Vec from elements in a Seq.
328	    *
329	    * For chisel3._, this operator is mono-directioned; all sub-elements of `this` will be driven by sub-elements of `that`.
330	    *  - Equivalent to `this :#= that`
331	    *
332	    * For Chisel._, this operator connections bi-directionally via emitting the FIRRTL.<=
333	    *  - Equivalent to `this :<>= that`

335	    * @note the length of this Vec must match the length of the input Seq
336	    * @group connection

339	    require(
340	      this.length == that.length,
341	      s"Cannot assign to a Vec of length ${this.length} from a Seq of different length ${that.length}"
342	    )
343	    for ((a, b) <- this.zip(that))

347	  /** "The strong connect operator", assigning elements in this Vec from elements in a Vec.
348	    *
349	    * For chisel3._, this operator is mono-directioned; all sub-elements of `this` will be driven by sub-elements of `that`.
350	    *  - Equivalent to `this :#= that`
351	    *
352	    * For Chisel._, this operator connections bi-directionally via emitting the FIRRTL.<=
353	    *  - Equivalent to `this :<>= that`, with the additional restriction that the relative bundle field flips must match
354	    *
355	    * @note This is necessary in [[Aggregate]], rather than relying on [[Data.:=]], due to supporting the Seq
356	    * @note the length of this Vec must match the length of the input Vec
357	    * @group connection

361	  private[chisel3] def _applyImpl(p: UInt)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): T = {
362	    requireIsHardware(this, "vec")
363	    requireIsHardware(p, "vec index")
364	
365	    // Don't bother with complex dynamic indexing logic when the index is a literal and therefore static
366	    // We also don't want to warn on literals that are "too small"
367	    p.litOption match {
368	      case Some(idx) if idx < length => return this.apply(idx.intValue)
369	      case _                         => // Fall through to control flow below
370	    }
371	
372	    if (length == 0) {
373	      Builder.warning(Warning(WarningID.ExtractFromVecSizeZero, s"Cannot extract from Vec of size 0."))
374	    } else {
375	      p.widthOption.foreach { pWidth =>
376	        val correctWidth = BigInt(length - 1).bitLength
377	        def mkMsg(msg: String): String =
378	          s"Dynamic index with width $pWidth is too $msg for Vec of size $length (expected index width $correctWidth)."
379	
380	        if (pWidth > correctWidth) {
381	          Builder.warning(Warning(WarningID.DynamicIndexTooWide, mkMsg("wide")))
382	        } else if (pWidth < correctWidth) {
383	          Builder.warning(Warning(WarningID.DynamicIndexTooNarrow, mkMsg("narrow")))
384	        }
385	      }
386	    }
387	
388	    // Special handling for views
389	    if (isView(this)) {
390	      reifyIdentityView(this) match {
391	        // Views complicate things a bit, but views that correspond exactly to an identical Vec can just forward the
392	        // dynamic indexing to the target Vec
393	        // In theory, we could still do this forwarding if the sample element were different by deriving a DataView
394	        case Some((target: Vec[T @unchecked], _))
395	            if this.length == target.length &&
396	              this.sample_element.typeEquivalent(target.sample_element) =>
397	          return target.apply(p)
398	        case _ => throw InvalidViewException("Dynamic indexing of Views is not yet supported")
399	      }
400	    }
401	
402	    val port = gen
403	
404	    // Reconstruct the resolvedDirection (in Aggregate.bind), since it's not stored.
405	    // It may not be exactly equal to that value, but the results are the same.
406	    val reconstructedResolvedDirection = direction match {
407	      case ActualDirection.Input  => SpecifiedDirection.Input
408	      case ActualDirection.Output => SpecifiedDirection.Output
409	      case ActualDirection.Bidirectional.Default | ActualDirection.Unspecified =>
410	        SpecifiedDirection.Unspecified
411	      case ActualDirection.Bidirectional.Flipped => SpecifiedDirection.Flip
412	      case ActualDirection.Empty                 => SpecifiedDirection.Unspecified
413	      case dir                                   => throwException(s"Unexpected directionality: $dir")
414	    }
415	    // TODO port technically isn't directly child of this data structure, but the result of some
416	    // muxes / demuxes. However, this does make access consistent with the top-level bindings.
417	    // Perhaps there's a cleaner way of accomplishing this...
418	    port.bind(DynamicIndexBinding(this), reconstructedResolvedDirection)
419	
420	    val i = Vec.truncateIndex(p, length)(UnlocatableSourceInfo)
421	    port.setRef(Node(this), i)
422	
423	    port
424	  }

449	  private[chisel3] def _reduceTreeImpl(
450	    redOp:   (T, T) => T,
451	    layerOp: (T) => T = (x: T) => x
452	  )(
453	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
454	  ): T = {
455	    require(!isEmpty, "Cannot apply reduction on a vec of size 0")
456	
457	    def recReduce[T](s: Seq[T], op: (T, T) => T, lop: (T) => T): T = {
458	
459	      val n = s.length
460	      n match {
461	        case 1 => lop(s(0))
462	        case 2 => op(s(0), s(1))
463	        case _ =>
464	          val m = pow(2, floor(log10(n - 1) / log10(2))).toInt // number of nodes in next level, will be a power of 2
465	          val p = 2 * m - n // number of nodes promoted
466	
467	          val l = s.take(p).map(lop)
468	          val r = s
469	            .drop(p)
470	            .grouped(2)
471	            .map { case Seq(a, b) =>
472	              op(a, b)
473	            }
474	            .toVector
475	          recReduce(l ++ r, op, lop)
476	      }
477	    }
478	
479	    recReduce(this, redOp, layerOp)
480	  }

482	  /** Creates a Vec literal of this type with specified values. this must be a chisel type.
483	    *
484	    * @param elementInitializers literal values, specified as a pair of the Vec field to the literal value.
485	    * The Vec field is specified as a function from an object of this type to the field.
486	    * Fields that aren't initialized to DontCare, and assignment to a wire will overwrite any
487	    * existing value with DontCare.
488	    * @return a Vec literal of this type with subelement values specified
489	    *
490	    * Vec(2, UInt(8.W)).Lit(
491	    *   1 -> 0x0A.U,
492	    *   2 -> 0x0B.U
493	    * )
494	    * }}}
495	    */
496	  private[chisel3] def _makeLit(
497	    elementInitializers: (Int, T)*
498	  )(
499	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
500	  ): this.type = {
501	
502	    def checkLiteralConstruction(): Unit = {
503	      val dupKeys = elementInitializers.map { x => x._1 }.groupBy(x => x).flatMap { case (k, v) =>
504	        if (v.length > 1) {
505	          Some(k, v.length)
506	        } else {
507	          None
508	        }
509	      }
510	      if (dupKeys.nonEmpty) {
511	        throw new VecLiteralException(
512	          s"VecLiteral: has duplicated indices ${dupKeys.map { case (k, n) => s"$k($n times)" }.mkString(",")}"
513	        )
514	      }
515	
516	      val outOfRangeIndices = elementInitializers.map(_._1).filter { case index => index < 0 || index >= length }
517	      if (outOfRangeIndices.nonEmpty) {
518	        throw new VecLiteralException(
519	          s"VecLiteral: The following indices (${outOfRangeIndices.mkString(",")}) " +
520	            s"are less than zero or greater or equal to than Vec length"
521	        )
522	      }
523	
524	      // look for literals of this vec that are wider than the vec's type
525	      val badLits = elementInitializers.flatMap {
526	        case (index, lit) =>
527	          (sample_element.width, lit.width) match {
528	            case (KnownWidth(m), KnownWidth(n)) =>
529	              if (m < n) Some(index -> lit) else None
530	            case (KnownWidth(_), _) =>
531	              None
532	            case (UnknownWidth, _) =>
533	              None
534	            case _ =>
535	              Some(index -> lit)
536	          }
537	        case _ => None
538	      }
539	      if (badLits.nonEmpty) {
540	        throw new VecLiteralException(
541	          s"VecLiteral: Vec[$gen] has the following incorrectly typed or sized initializers: " +
542	            badLits.map { case (a, b) => s"$a -> $b" }.mkString(",")
543	        )
544	      }
545	
546	    }
547	
548	    requireIsChiselType(this, "vec literal constructor model")
549	    checkLiteralConstruction()
550	
551	    val clone: this.type = this.cloneType
552	    val cloneFields = getRecursiveFields(clone, "(vec root)").toMap
553	
554	    // Create the Vec literal binding from litArgs of arguments
555	    val vecLitLinkedMap = new mutable.LinkedHashMap[Data, LitArg]()
556	    elementInitializers.sortBy { case (a, _) => a }.foreach { case (fieldIndex, value) =>
557	      val field = clone.apply(fieldIndex)
558	      val fieldName = cloneFields.getOrElse(
559	        field,
560	        throw new VecLiteralException(
561	          s"field $field (with value $value) is not a field," +
562	            s" ensure the field is specified as a function returning a field on an object of class ${this.getClass}," +
563	            s" eg '_.a' to select hypothetical bundle field 'a'"
564	        )
565	      )
566	
567	      val valueBinding = value.topBindingOpt match {
568	        case Some(litBinding: LitBinding) => litBinding
569	        case _ => throw new VecLiteralException(s"field $fieldIndex specified with non-literal value $value")
570	      }
571	
572	      field match { // Get the litArg(s) for this field
573	        case bitField: Bits =>
574	          if (!field.typeEquivalent(bitField)) {
575	            throw new VecLiteralException(
576	              s"VecLit: Literal specified at index $fieldIndex ($value) does not match Vec type $sample_element"
577	            )
578	          }
579	          if (bitField.getWidth > field.getWidth) {
580	            throw new VecLiteralException(
581	              s"VecLit: Literal specified at index $fieldIndex ($value) is too wide for Vec type $sample_element"
582	            )
583	          }
584	          val litArg = valueBinding match {
585	            case ElementLitBinding(litArg) => litArg
586	            case BundleLitBinding(litMap) =>
587	              litMap.getOrElse(
588	                value,
589	                throw new BundleLiteralException(s"Field $fieldName specified with unspecified value")
590	              )
591	            case VecLitBinding(litMap) =>
592	              litMap.getOrElse(
593	                value,
594	                throw new VecLiteralException(s"Field $fieldIndex specified with unspecified value")
595	              )
596	          }
597	          val adjustedLitArg = litArg.cloneWithWidth(sample_element.width)
598	          vecLitLinkedMap(bitField) = adjustedLitArg
599	
600	        case recordField: Record =>
601	          if (!(recordField.typeEquivalent(value))) {
602	            throw new VecLiteralException(
603	              s"field $fieldIndex $recordField specified with non-type-equivalent value $value"
604	            )
605	          }
606	          // Copy the source BundleLitBinding with fields (keys) remapped to the clone
607	          val remap = getMatchedFields(value, recordField).toMap
608	          valueBinding.asInstanceOf[BundleLitBinding].litMap.map { case (valueField, valueValue) =>
609	            vecLitLinkedMap(remap(valueField)) = valueValue
610	          }
611	
612	        case vecField: Vec[_] =>
613	          if (!(vecField.typeEquivalent(value))) {
614	            throw new VecLiteralException(
615	              s"field $fieldIndex $vecField specified with non-type-equivalent value $value"
616	            )
617	          }
618	          // Copy the source VecLitBinding with vecFields (keys) remapped to the clone
619	          val remap = getMatchedFields(value, vecField).toMap
620	          value.topBinding.asInstanceOf[VecLitBinding].litMap.map { case (valueField, valueValue) =>
621	            vecLitLinkedMap(remap(valueField)) = valueValue
622	          }
623	
624	        case enumField: EnumType => {
625	          if (!(enumField.typeEquivalent(value))) {
626	            throw new VecLiteralException(
627	              s"field $fieldIndex $enumField specified with non-type-equivalent enum value $value"
628	            )
629	          }
630	          val litArg = valueBinding match {
631	            case ElementLitBinding(litArg) => litArg
632	            case _ =>
633	              throw new VecLiteralException(s"field $fieldIndex $enumField could not bematched with $valueBinding")
634	          }
635	          vecLitLinkedMap(field) = litArg
636	        }
637	
638	        case _ => throw new VecLiteralException(s"unsupported field $fieldIndex of type $field")
639	      }
640	    }
641	
642	    clone.bind(VecLitBinding(VectorMap(vecLitLinkedMap.toSeq: _*)))
643	    clone
644	  }
645	}

654	    // collection API. However, a deprecation phase isn't possible, since
655	    // changing apply(elt0, elts*) to apply(elts*) causes a function collision
656	    // with apply(Seq) after type erasure. Workarounds by either introducing a
657	    // DummyImplicit or additional type parameter will break some code.

660	    require(elts.nonEmpty, "Vec hardware values are not allowed to be empty")

663	    val vec = Wire(Vec(elts.length, cloneSupertype(elts, "Vec")))
664	
665	    for ((lhs, rhs) <- vec.zip(elts)) {
666	      lhs :<>= rhs
667	    }

679	    _applyImpl((0 until n).map(i => gen(i)))

681	  protected def _tabulateImpl[T <: Data](
682	    n: Int,
683	    m: Int
684	  )(gen: (Int, Int) => T)(
685	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
686	  ): Vec[Vec[T]] = {
687	    // TODO make this lazy (requires LazyList and cross compilation, beyond the scope of this PR)
688	    val elts = Seq.tabulate(n, m)(gen)
689	    val flatElts = elts.flatten
690	
691	    require(flatElts.nonEmpty, "Vec hardware values are not allowed to be empty")
692	    flatElts.foreach(requireIsHardware(_, "vec element"))
693	
694	    val tpe = cloneSupertype(flatElts, "Vec.tabulate")
695	    val myVec = Wire(Vec(n, Vec(m, tpe)))
696	    for {
697	      (xs1D, ys1D) <- myVec.zip(elts)
698	      (lhs, rhs) <- xs1D.zip(ys1D)
699	    } {
700	      lhs :<>= rhs
701	    }
702	    myVec
703	  }

705	  protected def _tabulateImpl[T <: Data](
706	    n: Int,
707	    m: Int,
708	    p: Int
709	  )(gen: (Int, Int, Int) => T)(
710	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
711	  ): Vec[Vec[Vec[T]]] = {
712	    // TODO make this lazy (requires LazyList and cross compilation, beyond the scope of this PR)
713	    val elts = Seq.tabulate(n, m, p)(gen)
714	    val flatElts = elts.flatten.flatten
715	
716	    require(flatElts.nonEmpty, "Vec hardware values are not allowed to be empty")
717	    flatElts.foreach(requireIsHardware(_, "vec element"))
718	
719	    val tpe = cloneSupertype(flatElts, "Vec.tabulate")
720	    val myVec = Wire(Vec(n, Vec(m, Vec(p, tpe))))
721	
722	    for {
723	      (xs2D, ys2D) <- myVec.zip(elts)
724	      (xs1D, ys1D) <- xs2D.zip(ys2D)
725	      (lhs, rhs) <- xs1D.zip(ys1D)
726	    } {
727	      lhs :<>= rhs
728	    }
729	
730	    myVec
731	  }

811	    *       and is meant for internal Chisel use only. Can not be manually overridden by users, or else an error will be thrown.

822	  private def checkClone(clone: Record): Unit = {
823	    for ((name, field) <- _elements) {
824	      if (clone._elements(name) eq field) {
825	        throw new AutoClonetypeException(
826	          s"The bundle plugin was unable to clone $clone that has field '$name' aliased with base $this." +
827	            "This likely happened because you tried nesting Data arguments inside of other data structures." +
828	            " Try wrapping the field(s) in Input(...), Output(...), or Flipped(...) if appropriate." +
829	            " As a last resort, you can call chisel3.reflect.DataMirror.internal.chiselTypeClone on any nested Data arguments." +
830	            " See the cookbook entry 'How do I deal with the \"unable to clone\" error?' for more details."

836	  override def cloneType: this.type = {

842	  // Doing this earlier than onModuleClose allows field names to be available for prefixing the names
843	  // of hardware created when connecting to one of these elements
844	  private def setElementRefs(): Unit = {
845	    val opaqueType = this._isOpaqueType
846	    require(
847	      !opaqueType || (_elements.size == 1 && _elements.head._1 == ""),
848	      s"Opaque types must have exactly one element with an empty name, not ${_elements.size}: ${elements.keys.mkString(", ")}"
849	    )
850	    val thisNode = Node(this) // Share the same Node for all elements.
851	    // Names of _elements have already been namespaced (and therefore sanitized)

857	  /** Checks that there are no duplicate elements (aka aliased fields) in the Record */
858	  private def checkForAndReportDuplicates(): Unit = {
859	    // Using List to avoid allocation in the common case of no duplicates
860	    var duplicates: List[Data] = Nil
861	    // Is there a more optimized datastructure we could use with the Int identities? BitSet? Requires benchmarking.
862	    val seen = mutable.HashSet.empty[Data]
863	    this.elementsIterator.foreach { e =>
864	      if (seen(e)) {
865	        duplicates = e :: duplicates
866	      }
867	      seen += e
868	    }
869	    if (!duplicates.isEmpty) {
870	      // show groups of names of fields with duplicate id's
871	      // The sorts make the displayed order of fields deterministic and matching the order of occurrence in the Bundle.
872	      // It's a bit convoluted but happens rarely and makes the error message easier to understand
873	      val dupNames = duplicates.toSeq
874	        .sortBy(_._id)
875	        .map { duplicate =>
876	          this._elements.collect { case x if x._2._id == duplicate._id => x }.toSeq
877	            .sortBy(_._2._id)
878	            .map(_._1)
879	            .reverse
880	            .mkString("(", ",", ")")
881	        }
882	        .mkString(",")
883	      throw new AliasedAggregateFieldException(
884	        s"${this.className} contains aliased fields named ${dupNames}"
885	      )
886	    }
887	  }

889	  /* Tracking variable for deciding Record flipped-ness. */
890	  private[chisel3] var _containsAFlipped: Boolean = false
891	
892	  /* In the context of Records, containsAFlipped is assigned true if any of its children are flipped. */
893	  override def containsAFlipped: Boolean = _containsAFlipped
894	
895	  override private[chisel3] def bind(target: Binding, parentDirection: SpecifiedDirection): Unit = {
896	    this.maybeAddToParentIds(target)
897	    binding = target
898	
899	    val resolvedDirection = SpecifiedDirection.fromParent(parentDirection, specifiedDirection)
900	
901	    checkForAndReportDuplicates()
902	
903	    // Share same object for binding of all children.
904	    val childBinding = ChildBinding(this)
905	    for (((_, child), sameChild) <- this.elements.iterator.zip(this.elementsIterator)) {
906	      // This check is for making sure that elements always returns the
907	      // same object, which will not be the case if the user makes it a
908	      // def inside the Record. Checking elementsIterator against itself
909	      // is not useful for this check because it's a lazy val which will
910	      // always return the same thing.
911	      if (child != sameChild) {
912	        throwException(
913	          s"${this.className} does not return the same objects when calling .elements multiple times. Did you make it a def by mistake?"
914	        )
915	      }
916	      child.bind(childBinding, resolvedDirection)
917	
918	      // Update the flipped tracker based on the flipped-ness of this specific child element
919	      _containsAFlipped |= child.containsAFlipped
920	    }
921	
922	    // Check that children obey the directionality rules.
923	    val childDirections = elementsIterator.map(_.direction).toSet - ActualDirection.Empty
924	    direction = ActualDirection.fromChildren(childDirections, resolvedDirection) match {
925	      case Some(dir) => dir
926	      case None =>
927	        throwException(s"Internal Error! Unhandled directionality of children: $childDirections for $this!")
928	    }
929	    setElementRefs()
930	
931	    this match {
932	      case aliasedRecord: HasTypeAlias => Builder.setRecordAlias(aliasedRecord, resolvedDirection)
933	      case _ =>
934	    }
935	  }

937	  /** Creates a Bundle literal of this type with specified values. this must be a chisel type.
938	    *
939	    * @param elems literal values, specified as a pair of the Bundle field to the literal value.
940	    * The Bundle field is specified as a function from an object of this type to the field.
941	    * Fields that aren't initialized to DontCare, and assignment to a wire will overwrite any
942	    * existing value with DontCare.
943	    * @return a Bundle literal of this type with subelement values specified
944	    *
945	    * @example {{{
946	    * class MyBundle extends Bundle {
947	    *   val a = UInt(8.W)
948	    *   val b = Bool()
949	    * }
950	    *
951	    * (new MyBundle).Lit(
952	    *   _.a -> 42.U,
953	    *   _.b -> true.B
954	    * )
955	    * }}}
956	    */
957	  private[chisel3] def _makeLit(elems: (this.type => (Data, Data))*)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): this.type = {
958	
959	    requireIsChiselType(this, "bundle literal constructor model")
960	    val clone: this.type = cloneType
961	    val cloneFields = getRecursiveFields(clone, "_").toMap
962	
963	    // Create the Bundle literal binding from litargs of arguments
964	    val bundleLitMapping = elems.map { fn => fn(clone) }.flatMap { case (field, value) =>
965	      val fieldName = cloneFields.getOrElse(
966	        field,
967	        throw new BundleLiteralException(
968	          s"field $field (with value $value) is not a field," +
969	            s" ensure the field is specified as a function returning a field on an object of class ${this.getClass}," +
970	            s" eg '_.a' to select hypothetical bundle field 'a'"
971	        )
972	      )
973	      val valueBinding = value.topBindingOpt match {
974	        case Some(litBinding: LitBinding) => litBinding
975	        case _ => throw new BundleLiteralException(s"field $fieldName specified with non-literal value $value")
976	      }
977	
978	      field match { // Get the litArg(s) for this field
979	        case field: Bits =>
980	          if (field.getClass != value.getClass) { // TODO typeEquivalent is too strict because it checks width
981	            throw new BundleLiteralException(
982	              s"Field $fieldName $field specified with non-type-equivalent value $value"
983	            )
984	          }
985	          val litArg = valueBinding match {
986	            case ElementLitBinding(litArg) => litArg
987	            case BundleLitBinding(litMap) =>
988	              litMap.getOrElse(
989	                value,
990	                throw new BundleLiteralException(s"Field $fieldName specified with unspecified value")
991	              )
992	            case VecLitBinding(litMap) =>
993	              litMap.getOrElse(
994	                value,
995	                throw new VecLiteralException(s"Vec literal $fieldName specified with out literal values")
996	              )
997	
998	          }
999	          Seq(field -> litArg)
1000	
1001	        case field: Record =>
1002	          if (!(field.typeEquivalent(value))) {
1003	            throw new BundleLiteralException(
1004	              s"field $fieldName $field specified with non-type-equivalent value $value"
1005	            )
1006	          }
1007	          // Copy the source BundleLitBinding with fields (keys) remapped to the clone
1008	          val remap = getMatchedFields(value, field).toMap
1009	          value.topBinding.asInstanceOf[BundleLitBinding].litMap.map { case (valueField, valueValue) =>
1010	            remap(valueField) -> valueValue
1011	          }
1012	
1013	        case vecField: Vec[_] =>
1014	          if (!(vecField.typeEquivalent(value))) {
1015	            throw new BundleLiteralException(
1016	              s"field $fieldName $vecField specified with non-type-equivalent value $value"
1017	            )
1018	          }
1019	          // Copy the source BundleLitBinding with fields (keys) remapped to the clone
1020	          val remap = getMatchedFields(value, vecField).toMap
1021	          value.topBinding.asInstanceOf[VecLitBinding].litMap.map { case (valueField, valueValue) =>
1022	            remap(valueField) -> valueValue
1023	          }
1024	
1025	        case field: EnumType => {
1026	          if (!(field.typeEquivalent(value))) {
1027	            throw new BundleLiteralException(
1028	              s"field $fieldName $field specified with non-type-equivalent enum value $value"
1029	            )
1030	          }
1031	          val litArg = valueBinding match {
1032	            case ElementLitBinding(litArg) => litArg
1033	            case _ =>
1034	              throw new BundleLiteralException(s"field $fieldName $field could not be matched with $valueBinding")
1035	          }
1036	          Seq(field -> litArg)
1037	        }
1038	        case _ => throw new BundleLiteralException(s"unsupported field $fieldName of type $field")
1039	      }
1040	    }
1041	
1042	    // don't convert to a Map yet to preserve duplicate keys
1043	    val duplicates = bundleLitMapping.map(_._1).groupBy(identity).collect { case (x, elts) if elts.size > 1 => x }
1044	    if (!duplicates.isEmpty) {
1045	      val duplicateNames = duplicates.map(cloneFields(_)).mkString(", ")
1046	      throw new BundleLiteralException(s"duplicate fields $duplicateNames in Bundle literal constructor")
1047	    }
1048	    // Check widths and sign extend as appropriate.
1049	    val bundleLitMap = bundleLitMapping.view.map { case (field, value) =>
1050	      field.width match {
1051	        // If width is unknown, then it is set by the literal value.
1052	        case UnknownWidth => field -> value
1053	        case width @ KnownWidth(widthValue) =>
1054	          val valuex = if (widthValue < value.width.get) {
1055	            // For legacy reasons, 0.U is 1-bit, don't warn when it comes up as a literal value for 0-bit Bundle lit field.
1056	            val dontWarnOnZeroDotU = widthValue == 0 && value.num == 0 && value.width.get == 1
1057	            if (!dontWarnOnZeroDotU) {
1058	              val msg = s"Literal value $value is too wide for field ${cloneFields(field)} with width $widthValue"
1059	              Builder.warning(Warning(WarningID.BundleLiteralValueTooWide, msg))
1060	            }
1061	            // Mask the value to the width of the field.
1062	            val mask = (BigInt(1) << widthValue) - 1
1063	            value.cloneWithValue(value.num & mask).cloneWithWidth(width)
1064	          } else if (widthValue > value.width.get) value.cloneWithWidth(width)
1065	          // Otherwise, ensure width is same as that of the field.
1066	          else value
1067	
1068	          field -> valuex
1069	      }
1070	    }.toMap
1071	    clone.bind(BundleLitBinding(bundleLitMap))
1072	    clone
1073	  }

1075	  /** The collection of [[chisel3.Data]]
1076	    *
1077	    * This underlying datastructure is a ListMap because the elements must
1078	    * remain ordered for serialization/deserialization. Elements added later
1079	    * are higher order when serialized (this is similar to `Vec`). For example:
1080	    * {{{
1081	    *   // Assume we have some type MyRecord that creates a Record from the ListMap
1082	    *   val record = MyRecord(ListMap("fizz" -> UInt(16.W), "buzz" -> UInt(16.W)))
1083	    *   // "buzz" is higher order because it was added later than "fizz"
1084	    *   record("fizz") := "hdead".U
1085	    *   record("buzz") := "hbeef".U
1086	    *   val uint = record.asUInt
1087	    *   assert(uint === "hbeefdead".U) // This will pass
1088	    * }}}
1089	    */
1090	  override def toString: String = {
1091	    topBindingOpt match {
1092	      case Some(BundleLitBinding(_)) =>
1093	        val contents = _elements.toList.reverse.map { case (name, data) =>
1094	          s"$name=$data"
1095	        }.mkString(", ")
1096	        s"$className($contents)"
1097	      case _ => stringAccessor(s"$className")
1098	    }
1099	  }
1100	
1101	  def elements: SeqMap[String, Data]
1102	
1103	  // Internal representation of Record elements. _elements makes it
1104	  // possible to check for rebinding issues with Record elements
1105	  // without having to recurse over all elements after the Record is
1106	  // constructed. Laziness of _elements means that this check will
1107	  // occur (only) at the first instance _elements is referenced.
1108	  // Also used to sanitize names and convert to more optimized VectorMap datastructure (if necessary)
1109	  // TODO We should figure out how to combine this with elements
1110	  // The main trick there is storing the sanitized names and making sure every Chisel API uses them
1111	  // Strawman proposal is to put sanitized name in the Slot refs of the children, and then replace
1112	  // _elements and elements with a single Array and deprecate elements as a public API
1113	  // This would also give an opportunity to stop storing elements in reverse order
1114	  private[chisel3] lazy val _elements: VectorMap[String, Data] = {
1115	    // Since elements is a map, it is impossible for two elements to have the same
1116	    // identifier; however, Namespace sanitizes identifiers to make them legal for Firrtl/Verilog
1117	    // which can cause collisions
1118	    // Note that OpaqueTypes cannot have sanitization (the name of the element needs to stay empty)
1119	    //   Use an empty Namespace to indicate OpaqueType
1120	    val namespace = Option.when(!this._isOpaqueType)(Namespace.empty)
1121	    val originalElements = elements
1122	    // Don't create a new map unless necessary, this is much more memory efficient in common case
1123	    // This is true if elements is not a VectorMap or if any names need sanitization
1124	    var needNewMap = !originalElements.isInstanceOf[VectorMap[_, _]]
1125	    val newNames =
1126	      elements.view.map { case (name, field) =>
1127	        if (field.binding.isDefined) {
1128	          throw RebindingException(
1129	            s"Cannot create Record ${this.className}; element ${field} of Record must be a Chisel type, not hardware."
1130	          )
1131	        }
1132	        // namespace.name also sanitizes for firrtl, leave name alone for OpaqueTypes
1133	        val sanitizedName = namespace.map(_.name(name, leadingDigitOk = true)).getOrElse(name)
1134	        if (sanitizedName != name) {
1135	          needNewMap = true
1136	        }
1137	        sanitizedName
1138	      }.toArray // It's very important we eagerly evaluate this sequence.
1139	    if (needNewMap) {
1140	      newNames.view
1141	        .zip(originalElements)
1142	        .map { case (name, (_, data)) => name -> data }
1143	        .to(VectorMap) // VectorMap has O(1) lookup whereas ListMap is O(n)
1144	    } else {
1145	      originalElements.asInstanceOf[VectorMap[String, Data]]
1146	    }
1147	  }

1222	  * members variables of [[Data]] subtypes to be elements in the Bundle.
1223	  *
1224	  * Example of an anonymous IO bundle

1226	  *   class MyModule extends Module {
1227	  *     val io = IO(new Bundle {
1228	  *       val in = Input(UInt(64.W))
1229	  *       val out = Output(SInt(128.W))
1230	  *     })
1231	  *   }
1232	  * }}}
1233	  *
1234	  * Or as a named class
1235	  * {{{
1236	  *   class Packet extends Bundle {
1237	  *     val header = UInt(16.W)
1238	  *     val addr   = UInt(16.W)
1239	  *     val data   = UInt(32.W)
1240	  *   }

1242	  *     val inPacket = IO(Input(new Packet))
1243	  *     val outPacket = IO(Output(new Packet))
1244	  *     val reg = Reg(new Packet)
1245	  *     reg := inPacket
1246	  *     outPacket := reg
1247	  *   }
1248	  * }}}

1250	  * The fields of a Bundle are stored in an ordered Map called "elements" in reverse order of
1251	  * definition
1252	  * {{{
1253	  *   class MyBundle extends Bundle {
1254	  *     val foo = UInt(8.W)
1255	  *     val bar = UInt(8.W)
1256	  *   }
1257	  *   val wire = Wire(new MyBundle)

1259	  * }}}

1270	      case name if name.startsWith("$anon$") => "AnonymousBundle" // fallback for anonymous Bundle case
1271	      case ""                                => "AnonymousBundle" // ditto, but on other platforms
1272	      case name                              => name
1273	    }
1274	  } catch {
1275	    // This happens if you have nested objects which your class is defined in
1276	    case e: java.lang.InternalError if e.getMessage == "Malformed class name" => this.getClass.toString

1279	  /** The collection of [[Data]]
1280	    *
1281	    * Elements defined earlier in the Bundle are higher order upon
1282	    * serialization. For example:
1283	    * @example
1284	    * {{{
1285	    *   class MyBundle extends Bundle {
1286	    *     val foo = UInt(16.W)
1287	    *     val bar = UInt(16.W)
1288	    *   }

1300	  // checking for errors and filtering out mistakes
1301	  private def _processRawElements(rawElements: Iterable[(String, Any)]): SeqMap[String, Data] = {
1302	    val hardwareFields = rawElements.flatMap {
1303	      case (name, data: Data) =>
1304	        if (data.isSynthesizable) {
1305	          Some(s"$name: $data")
1306	        } else {
1307	          None
1308	        }
1309	      case (name, Some(data: Data)) =>
1310	        if (data.isSynthesizable) {
1311	          Some(s"$name: $data")
1312	        } else {
1313	          None
1314	        }
1315	      case (name, s: scala.collection.Seq[Any]) if s.nonEmpty =>

1318	          case d: Data =>
1319	            throwException(

1321	                s"(found public Seq member '${name}'). " +
1322	                "Either use a Vec if all elements are of the same type, or MixedVec if the elements " +

1328	        None

1338	      case (name, Some(data: Data)) =>

1340	      case _ => None

1342	  }

1344	  /** This method is implemented by the compiler plugin

1367	    * @note The order is reversed from the order of elements in order to print
1368	    *   the fields in the order they were defined
1369	    */

31	private[chisel3] class Namespace(keywords: Set[String], separator: Char = '_') {
32	  // This HashMap is compressed, not every name in the namespace is present here.
33	  // If the same name is requested multiple times, it only takes 1 entry in the HashMap and the
34	  // value is incremented for each time the name is requested.
35	  // Names can be requested that collide with compressed sets of names, thus the algorithm for
36	  // checking if a name is present in the Namespace is more complex than just checking the HashMap,
37	  // see getIndex below.
38	  private val names = collection.mutable.HashMap[String, Long]()
39	  for (keyword <- keywords)
40	    names(keyword) = 1
41	
42	  @tailrec
43	  private def rename(n: String, index: Long): String = {
44	    val tryName = s"${n}${separator}${index}"
45	    if (names.contains(tryName)) {
46	      rename(n, index + 1)
47	    } else {
48	      names(n) = index + 1
49	      tryName
50	    }
51	  }
52	
53	  /** Checks if `n` ends in `_\d+` and returns the substring before `_` if so, null otherwise */
54	  // TODO can and should this be folded in to sanitize? Same iteration as the forall?
55	  private def prefix(n: String): Int = {
56	    // This is micro-optimized because it runs on every single name
57	    var i = n.size - 1
58	    while (i > 0 && n(i).isDigit) {
59	      i -= 1
60	    }
61	    // Will get i == 0 for all digits or _\d+ with empty prefix, those have no prefix so returning 0 is correct
62	    if (i >= (n.size - 1)) 0 // no digits
63	    else if (n(i) != separator) 0 // no _
64	    else i
65	  }
66	
67	  // Gets the current index for this name, None means it is not contained in the Namespace
68	  private def getIndex(elem: String): Option[Long] =
69	    names.get(elem).orElse {
70	      // This exact name isn't contained, but if we end in _<idx>, we need to check our prefix
71	      val maybePrefix = prefix(elem)
72	      if (maybePrefix == 0) None
73	      else {
74	        // If we get a prefix collision and our index is taken, we start disambiguating with _<idx>_1
75	        names
76	          .get(elem.take(maybePrefix))
77	          .filter { prefixIdx =>
78	            val ourIdx = elem.drop(maybePrefix + 1).toInt
79	            // The namespace starts disambiguating at _1 so _0 is a false collision case
80	            ourIdx != 0 && prefixIdx > ourIdx
81	          }
82	          .map(_ => 1)
83	      }
84	    }
85	
86	  def contains(elem: String): Boolean = getIndex(elem).isDefined
87	
88	  // leadingDigitOk is for use in fields of Records
89	  def name(elem: String, leadingDigitOk: Boolean = false): String = {
90	    val sanitized = sanitize(elem, leadingDigitOk)
91	    getIndex(sanitized) match {
92	      case Some(idx) => rename(sanitized, idx)
93	      case None =>
94	        names(sanitized) = 1
95	        sanitized
96	    }
97	  }
98	}
